Los hallazgos críticos muestran que los modelos fronterizos frecuentemente se involucran en comportamientos dañinos como chantaje y espionaje cuando se despliegan con acceso al sistema de archivos y correo electrónico.

title: Go Big or Go Home: Simulating Mobbing Behavior with Braitenbergian Robots
summary: Researchers simulated cooperative mobbing behavior in robots using light-based predators, exploring how communication range and group size affect coordination success. The work demonstrates principles applicable to designing autonomous agent control architectures based on biological swarm behaviors.
content:
arXiv:2604.00350v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We used the Webots robotics simulation platform to simulate a dyadic avoiding and mobbing predator behavior in a group of Braitenbergian robots. Mobbing is an antipredator adaptation used by some animals in which the individuals cooperatively attack or harass a predator to protect themselves. One way of coordinating a mobbing attack is using mobbing calls to summon other individuals of the mobbing species. We imitated this mechanism and simulated Braitenbergian robots that use mobbing calls when they face a light source (representing an inanimate predator) and mob it if they can summon allies, otherwise, they escape from it. We explore the effects of range of mobbing call (infinite range, mid-range and low-range) and the size of the robot group (ten robots vs three) on the overall success of mobbing. Our results suggest that both variables have significant impacts. This work has implications for simulations of action selection in artificial life and designing control architectures for autonomous agents.

zh.title: 大步向前或回家：用布拉腾伯格机器人模拟群体骚扰行为
zh.summary: 研究人员使用基于光线的捕食者在机器人中模拟合作群体骚扰行为，探索通信范围和群体规模如何影响协调成功。这项工作展示了适用于基于生物群体行为设计自主代理控制架构的原理。

fr.title: Go Big or Go Home : Simulation du Comportement de Mob avec des Robots Braitenbergiens
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont simulé le comportement de mob coopératif chez les robots à l'aide de prédateurs basés sur la lumière, explorant comment la plage de communication et la taille du groupe affectent le succès de la coordination. Le travail démontre des principes applicables à la conception d'architectures de contrôle d'agents autonomes basées sur les comportements d'essaim biologiques.

de.title: Groß denken oder nach Hause gehen: Simulation von Mobbing-Verhalten mit Braitenberg-Robotern
de.summary: Forscher simulierten kooperatives Mobbing-Verhalten bei Robotern mit lichtemittierenden Raubentieren und untersuchten, wie Kommunikationsbereich und Gruppengröße den Koordinationserfolg beeinflussen. Die Arbeit demonstriert Prinzipien, die auf die Gestaltung autonomer Agentenkontrollarchitekturen basierend auf biologischem Schwarmverhalten anwendbar sind.

es.title: Ir a lo Grande o Ir a Casa: Simulación del Comportamiento de Turba con Robots Braitenbergianos
es.summary: Los investigadores simularon el comportamiento colectivo de multitud cooperativa en robots usando depredadores basados en luz, explorando cómo el rango de comunicación y el tamaño del grupo afectan el éxito de la coordinación. El trabajo demuestra principios aplicables al diseño de arquitecturas de control de agentes autónomos basadas en comportamientos de enjambre biológico.

title: RAGShield: Provenance-Verified Defense-in-Depth Against Knowledge Base Poisoning in Government Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
summary: RAGShield is a five-layer defense framework protecting government RAG systems from knowledge base poisoning attacks through cryptographic document attestation and provenance verification. The system achieves 0% attack success on standard attacks while maintaining NIST compliance.
content:
arXiv:2604.00387v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: RAG systems deployed across federal agencies for citizen-facing services are vulnerable to knowledge base poisoning attacks, where adversaries inject malicious documents to manipulate outputs. Recent work demonstrates that as few as 10 adversarial passages can achieve 98.2% retrieval success rates. We observe that RAG knowledge base poisoning is structurally analogous to software supply chain attacks, and propose RAGShield, a five-layer defense-in-depth framework applying supply chain provenance verification to the RAG knowledge pipeline. RAGShield introduces: (1) C2PA-inspired cryptographic document attestation blocking unsigned and forged documents at ingestion; (2) trust-weighted retrieval prioritizing provenance-verified sources; (3) a formal taint lattice with cross-source contradiction detection catching insider threats even when provenance is valid; (4) provenance-aware generation with auditable citations; and (5) NIST SP 800-53 compliance mapping across 15 control families. Evaluation on a 500-passage Natural Questions corpus with 63 attack documents and 200 queries against five adversary tiers achieves 0.0% attack success rate including adaptive attacks (95% CI: [0.0%, 1.9%]) with 0.0% false positive rate. We honestly report that insider in-place replacement attacks achieve 17.5% ASR, identifying the fundamental limit of ingestion-time defense. The cross-source contradiction detector catches subtle numerical manipulation attacks that bypass provenance verification entirely.

zh.title: RAGShield：政府检索增强生成系统中知识库中毒的来源验证防御框架
zh.summary: RAGShield是一个五层防御框架，通过密码学文档证明和来源验证保护政府RAG系统免受知识库中毒攻击。该系统在标准攻击上实现了0%的攻击成功率，同时保持NIST合规性。

fr.title: RAGShield : Défense Vérifiée par Provenance en Profondeur Contre l'Empoisonnement de la Base de Connaissances dans les Systèmes de Génération Augmentée par Récupération Gouvernementale
fr.summary: RAGShield est un cadre de défense en cinq couches protégeant les systèmes RAG gouvernementaux contre les attaques d'empoisonnement de base de connaissances grâce à l'attestation de documents cryptographiques et à la vérification de la provenance. Le système atteint un taux de succès d'attaque de 0% sur les attaques standard tout en maintenant la conformité NIST.

de.title: RAGShield: Herkunftsverifizierte Verteidigung in die Tiefe gegen Wissensdatenbank-Vergiftung in behördlichen Retrieval-Augmented-Generation-Systemen
de.summary: RAGShield ist ein fünfschichtiges Verteidigungsrahmen, das behördliche RAG-Systeme vor Angriffen durch Wissensdatenbank-Vergiftung durch kryptografische Dokumentenbeglaubigung und Herkunftsverifizierung schützt. Das System erreicht eine Erfolgsquote von 0% bei Standardangriffen und behält gleichzeitig die NIST-Compliance bei.

es.title: RAGShield: Defensa Verificada por Procedencia en Profundidad Contra el Envenenamiento de Base de Conocimiento en Sistemas de Generación Aumentada por Recuperación Gubernamental
es.summary: RAGShield es un marco de defensa de cinco capas que protege los sistemas RAG gubernamentales de ataques de envenenamiento de base de conocimiento mediante atestación de documentos criptográficos y verificación de procedencia. El sistema logra una tasa de éxito de ataque del 0% en ataques estándar mientras mantiene el cumplimiento de NIST.

title: EvolveTool-Bench: Evaluating the Quality of LLM-Generated Tool Libraries as Software Artifacts
summary: EvolveTool-Bench introduces software quality metrics for evaluating LLM-generated tool libraries beyond task completion. The benchmark reveals that systems with similar task success rates differ by up to 18% in library health, highlighting software quality risks invisible to traditional evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2604.00392v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern LLM agents increasingly create their own tools at runtime -- from Python functions to API clients -- yet existing benchmarks evaluate them almost exclusively by downstream task completion. This is analogous to judging a software engineer only by whether their code runs, ignoring redundancy, regression, and safety. We introduce EvolveTool-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark for LLM-generated tool libraries in software engineering workflows. Across three domains requiring actual tool execution (proprietary data formats, API orchestration, and numerical computation), we define library-level software quality metrics -- reuse, redundancy, composition success, regression stability, and safety -- alongside a per-tool Tool Quality Score measuring correctness, robustness, generality, and code quality. In the first head-to-head comparison of code-level and strategy-level tool evolution (ARISE vs. EvoSkill vs. one-shot baselines, 99 tasks, two models), we show that systems with similar task completion (63-68%) differ by up to 18% in library health, revealing software quality risks invisible to task-only evaluation. Our results highlight that evaluation and governance of LLM-generated tools require treating the evolving tool library as a first-class software artifact, not a black box.

zh.title: EvolveTool-Bench：评估大型语言模型生成的工具库作为软件工件的质量
zh.summary: EvolveTool-Bench引入了软件质量指标，用于评估超越任务完成的大型语言模型生成的工具库。该基准表明具有相似任务成功率的系统在库健康度上相差高达18%，突出了传统评估看不见的软件质量风险。

fr.title: EvolveTool-Bench : Évaluer la Qualité des Bibliothèques d'Outils Générées par LLM en tant qu'Artefacts Logiciels
fr.summary: EvolveTool-Bench introduit des métriques de qualité logicielle pour évaluer les bibliothèques d'outils générées par LLM au-delà de l'accomplissement des tâches. L'indice de référence révèle que les systèmes avec des taux de succès de tâche similaires diffèrent de jusqu'à 18% en santé de bibliothèque, mettant en évidence les risques de qualité logicielle invisibles à l'évaluation traditionnelle.

de.title: EvolveTool-Bench: Bewertung der Qualität von LLM-generierten Tool-Bibliotheken als Softwareartefakte
de.summary: EvolveTool-Bench führt Softwarequalitätsmetriken zur Bewertung von LLM-generierten Tool-Bibliotheken über die Aufgabenvollendung hinaus ein. Der Benchmark zeigt, dass Systeme mit ähnlichen Aufgabenerfolgsquoten um bis zu 18% in der Bibliotheksgesundheit unterscheiden, was Softwarequalitätsrisiken hervorhebt, die für traditionelle Evaluationen unsichtbar sind.

es.title: EvolveTool-Bench: Evaluación de la Calidad de Bibliotecas de Herramientas Generadas por LLM como Artefactos de Software
es.summary: EvolveTool-Bench introduce métricas de calidad de software para evaluar bibliotecas de herramientas generadas por LLM más allá de la finalización de tareas. El benchmark revela que los sistemas con tasas de éxito de tareas similares difieren hasta en un 18% en la salud de la biblioteca, destacando riesgos de calidad de software invisibles para la evaluación tradicional.

title: Deep Networks Favor Simple Data
summary: This work demonstrates that deep neural networks systematically assign higher estimated density to simpler out-of-distribution data than complex in-distribution data, a phenomenon consistent across architectures and training objectives. The finding reveals fundamental biases in how neural networks process information.
content:
arXiv:2604.00394v2 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Estimated density is often interpreted as indicating how typical a sample is under a model. Yet deep models trained on one dataset can assign higher density to simpler out-of-distribution (OOD) data than to in-distribution test data. We refer to this behavior as the OOD anomaly. Prior work typically studies this phenomenon within a single architecture, detector, or benchmark, implicitly assuming certain canonical densities. We instead separate the trained network from the density estimator built from its representations or outputs. We introduce two estimators: Jacobian-based estimators and autoregressive self-estimators, making density analysis applicable to a wide range of models.
  Applying this perspective to a range of models, including iGPT, PixelCNN++, Glow, score-based diffusion models, DINOv2, and I-JEPA, we find the same striking regularity that goes beyond the OOD anomaly: lower-complexity samples receive higher estimated density, while higher-complexity samples receive lower estimated density. This ordering appears within a test set and across OOD pairs such as CIFAR-10 and SVHN, and remains highly consistent across independently trained models. To quantify these orderings, we introduce Spearman rank correlation and find striking agreement both across models and with external complexity metrics. Even when trained only on the lowest-density (most complex) samples - or even a single such sample - the resulting models still rank simpler images as higher density.
  These observations lead us beyond the original OOD anomaly to a more general conclusion: deep networks consistently favor simple data. Our goal is not to close this question, but to define and visualize it more clearly. We broaden its empirical scope and show that it appears across architectures, objectives, and density estimators.

zh.title: 深度网络偏向简单数据
zh.summary: 这项研究表明,深度神经网络系统地向更简单的分布外数据分配更高的估计密度,而不是向复杂的分布内数据分配密度,这种现象在不同的架构和训练目标中都是一致的。该发现揭示了神经网络处理信息的基本偏差。

fr.title: Les réseaux profonds favorisent les données simples
fr.summary: Ce travail démontre que les réseaux de neurones profonds attribuent systématiquement une densité estimée plus élevée aux données plus simples en dehors de la distribution qu'aux données complexes dans la distribution, un phénomène cohérent dans les architectures et les objectifs d'entraînement. La découverte révèle des biais fondamentaux dans la manière dont les réseaux de neurones traitent l'information.

de.title: Tiefe Netzwerke bevorzugen einfache Daten
de.summary: Diese Arbeit zeigt, dass tiefe neuronale Netzwerke systematisch einfacheren Daten außerhalb der Verteilung eine höhere geschätzte Dichte zuweisen als komplexen Daten innerhalb der Verteilung, ein Phänomen, das über verschiedene Architekturen und Trainingsziele hinweg konsistent ist. Der Befund zeigt grundlegende Verzerrungen in der Art und Weise, wie neuronale Netzwerke Informationen verarbeiten.

es.title: Las redes profundas favorecen datos simples
es.summary: Este trabajo demuestra que las redes neuronales profundas asignan sistemáticamente una densidad estimada más alta a datos más simples fuera de la distribución que a datos complejos dentro de la distribución, un fenómeno consistente entre arquitecturas y objetivos de entrenamiento. El hallazgo revela sesgos fundamentales en cómo las redes neuronales procesan información.

title: Improving Generalization of Deep Learning for Brain Metastases Segmentation Across Institutions
summary: Researchers propose a VAE-MMD preprocessing pipeline to adapt brain tumor segmentation models across institutions with different imaging protocols and hardware. The approach improves segmentation accuracy by over 11% without requiring labeled data from target institutions, addressing deployment barriers.
content:
arXiv:2604.00397v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Background: Deep learning has demonstrated significant potential for automated brain metastases (BM) segmentation; however, models trained at a singular institution often exhibit suboptimal performance at various sites due to disparities in scanner hardware, imaging protocols, and patient demographics. The goal of this work is to create a domain adaptation framework that will allow for BM segmentation to be used across multiple institutions.
  Methods: We propose a VAE-MMD preprocessing pipeline that combines variational autoencoders (VAE) with maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) loss, incorporating skip connections and self-attention mechanisms alongside nnU-Net segmentation. The method was tested on 740 patients from four public databases: Stanford, UCSF, UCLM, and PKG, evaluated by domain classifier's accuracy, sensitivity, precision, F1/F2 scores, surface Dice (sDice), and 95th percentile Hausdorff distance (HD95).
  Results: VAE-MMD reduced domain classifier accuracy from 0.91 to 0.50, indicating successful feature alignment across institutions. Reconstructed volumes attained a PSNR greater than 36 dB, maintaining anatomical accuracy. The combined method raised the mean F1 by 11.1% (0.700 to 0.778), the mean sDice by 7.93% (0.7121 to 0.7686), and reduced the mean HD95 by 65.5% (11.33 to 3.91 mm) across all four centers compared to the baseline nnU-Net.
  Conclusions: VAE-MMD effectively diminishes cross-institutional data heterogeneity and enhances BM segmentation generalization across volumetric, detection, and boundary-level metrics without necessitating target-domain labels, thereby overcoming a significant obstacle to the clinical implementation of AI-assisted segmentation.

zh.title: 改进深度学习在跨机构脑转移瘤分割中的泛化能力
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一个VAE-MMD预处理管道来适应跨机构的脑肿瘤分割模型,这些机构具有不同的成像协议和硬件。该方法在不需要来自目标机构的标记数据的情况下,将分割准确性提高了超过11%,解决了部署障碍。

fr.title: Amélioration de la généralisation de l'apprentissage profond pour la segmentation des métastases cérébrales entre institutions
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent un pipeline de prétraitement VAE-MMD pour adapter les modèles de segmentation de tumeurs cérébrales entre les institutions ayant des protocoles d'imagerie et du matériel différents. L'approche améliore la précision de la segmentation de plus de 11% sans nécessiter de données étiquetées provenant des institutions cibles, résolvant les obstacles au déploiement.

de.title: Verbesserung der Verallgemeinerung des Deep Learning für die Segmentierung von Hirnmetastasen über Institutionen hinweg
de.summary: Forscher schlagen eine VAE-MMD-Vorverarbeitungspipeline vor, um Gehirntumorensegmentierungsmodelle über Institutionen mit unterschiedlichen Bildgebungsprotokollen und Hardware hinweg anzupassen. Das Verfahren verbessert die Segmentierungsgenauigkeit um über 11%, ohne dass markierte Daten von Zielinstitutionen erforderlich sind, und behebt damit Bereitstellungshindernisse.

es.title: Mejora de la generalización del aprendizaje profundo para la segmentación de metástasis cerebrales entre instituciones
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen un pipeline de preprocesamiento VAE-MMD para adaptar modelos de segmentación de tumores cerebrales entre instituciones con protocolos de imagen y hardware diferentes. El enfoque mejora la precisión de la segmentación en más del 11% sin requerir datos etiquetados de las instituciones objetivo, abordando las barreras de implementación.

title: COTTA: Context-Aware Transfer Adaptation for Trajectory Prediction in Autonomous Driving
summary: This study examines trajectory prediction model adaptation from U.S. to Korean driving environments, showing that selective fine-tuning of decoder layers while freezing encoders reduces prediction error by 66% compared to training from scratch. The findings provide practical strategies for geographic domain adaptation in autonomous driving.
content:
arXiv:2604.00402v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Developing robust models to accurately predict the trajectories of surrounding agents is fundamental to autonomous driving safety. However, most public datasets, such as the Waymo Open Motion Dataset and Argoverse, are collected in Western road environments and do not reflect the unique traffic patterns, infrastructure, and driving behaviors of other regions, including South Korea. This domain discrepancy leads to performance degradation when state-of-the-art models trained on Western data are deployed in different geographic contexts. In this work, we investigate the adaptability of Query-Centric Trajectory Prediction (QCNet) when transferred from U.S.-based data to Korean road environments. Using a Korean autonomous driving dataset, we compare four training strategies: zero-shot transfer, training from scratch, full fine-tuning, and encoder freezing. Experimental results demonstrate that leveraging pretrained knowledge significantly improves prediction performance. Specifically, selectively fine-tuning the decoder while freezing the encoder yields the best trade-off between accuracy and training efficiency, reducing prediction error by over 66% compared to training from scratch. This study provides practical insights into effective transfer learning strategies for deploying trajectory prediction models in new geographic domains.

zh.title: COTTA:自动驾驶中用于轨迹预测的上下文感知转移适应
zh.summary: 本研究检查了从美国到韩国驾驶环境的轨迹预测模型适应,表明选择性地微调解码器层同时冻结编码器,与从零开始训练相比,预测错误减少了66%。这些发现为自动驾驶中的地理领域适应提供了实用策略。

fr.title: COTTA : Adaptation de transfert sensible au contexte pour la prédiction de trajectoire en conduite autonome
fr.summary: Cette étude examine l'adaptation du modèle de prédiction de trajectoire des environnements de conduite américains aux environnements coréens, montrant que l'ajustement fin sélectif des couches de décodeur tout en gélant les encodeurs réduit l'erreur de prédiction de 66% par rapport à l'entraînement à partir de zéro. Les résultats fournissent des stratégies pratiques pour l'adaptation de domaine géographique en conduite autonome.

de.title: COTTA: Kontextbewusste Transferanpassung für Trajektorienvorhersage beim autonomen Fahren
de.summary: Diese Studie untersucht die Anpassung von Trajektorienvorhersagemodellen von US-amerikanischen auf koreanische Fahrumgebungen und zeigt, dass das selektive Fine-Tuning von Decoder-Schichten bei gleichzeitigem Einfrieren von Encodern den Vorhersagefehler um 66% im Vergleich zum Training von Grund auf reduziert. Die Ergebnisse bieten praktische Strategien für die geografische Domänenanpassung beim autonomen Fahren.

es.title: COTTA: Adaptación de transferencia consciente del contexto para la predicción de trayectoria en conducción autónoma
es.summary: Este estudio examina la adaptación del modelo de predicción de trayectoria desde entornos de conducción estadounidenses a coreanos, mostrando que el ajuste fino selectivo de las capas del decodificador mientras se congelan los codificadores reduce el error de predicción en un 66% en comparación con el entrenamiento desde cero. Los resultados proporcionan estrategias prácticas para la adaptación de dominio geográfico en conducción autónoma.

title: Learning Humanoid Navigation from Human Data
summary: Researchers introduced EgoNav, a system that enables humanoid robots to navigate unseen environments by learning from just 5 hours of human walking data without robot-specific training. The system uses diffusion models and visual memory to achieve real-time navigation with natural behaviors like obstacle avoidance and crowd navigation.
content:
arXiv:2604.00416v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present EgoNav, a system that enables a humanoid robot to traverse diverse, unseen environments by learning entirely from 5 hours of human walking data, with no robot data or finetuning. A diffusion model predicts distributions of plausible future trajectories conditioned on past trajectory, a 360 deg visual memory fusing color, depth, and semantics, and video features from a frozen DINOv3 backbone that capture appearance cues invisible to depth sensors. A hybrid sampling scheme achieves real-time inference in 10 denoising steps, and a receding-horizon controller selects paths from the predicted distribution. We validate EgoNav through offline evaluations, where it outperforms baselines in collision avoidance and multi-modal coverage, and through zero-shot deployment on a Unitree G1 humanoid across unseen indoor and outdoor environments. Behaviors such as waiting for doors to open, navigating around crowds, and avoiding glass walls emerge naturally from the learned prior. We will release the dataset and trained models. Our website: https://egonav.weizhuowang.com

zh.title: 从人类数据学习人形机器人导航
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了EgoNav系统，该系统使人形机器人能够通过仅学习5小时的人类行走数据而在未见过的环境中进行导航，无需机器人特定的训练。该系统使用扩散模型和视觉记忆来实现实时导航，具有自然的行为，如避障和人群导航。

fr.title: Apprentissage de la navigation pour robots humanoïdes à partir de données humaines
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont introduit EgoNav, un système qui permet aux robots humanoïdes de naviguer dans des environnements non vus en apprenant à partir de seulement 5 heures de données de marche humaine sans formation spécifique au robot. Le système utilise des modèles de diffusion et une mémoire visuelle pour obtenir une navigation en temps réel avec des comportements naturels comme l'évitement d'obstacles et la navigation en foule.

de.title: Erlernen der humanoiden Navigation aus menschlichen Daten
de.summary: Forscher führten EgoNav ein, ein System, das humanoiden Robotern ermöglicht, in unbekannten Umgebungen zu navigieren, indem sie nur aus 5 Stunden menschlicher Gehendaten lernen, ohne roboterspezifisches Training. Das System nutzt Diffusionsmodelle und visuelles Gedächtnis, um Echtzeit-Navigation mit natürlichen Verhaltensweisen wie Hindernisvermeidung und Menschenmengen-Navigation zu erreichen.

es.title: Aprendizaje de navegación de robots humanoides a partir de datos humanos
es.summary: Los investigadores introdujeron EgoNav, un sistema que permite a los robots humanoides navegar en entornos no vistos aprendiendo a partir de solo 5 horas de datos de caminata humana sin entrenamiento específico del robot. El sistema utiliza modelos de difusión y memoria visual para lograr navegación en tiempo real con comportamientos naturales como evitar obstáculos y navegación en multitudes.

title: G-Drift MIA: Membership Inference via Gradient-Induced Feature Drift in LLMs
summary: Researchers presented G-Drift MIA, a white-box membership inference method that determines whether data was used in LLM training by analyzing gradient-induced changes in internal representations. The method substantially outperforms existing attacks and reveals that memorized samples exhibit structurally different feature drift than non-members.
content:
arXiv:2604.00419v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are trained on massive web-scale corpora, raising growing concerns about privacy and copyright. Membership inference attacks (MIAs) aim to determine whether a given example was used during training. Existing LLM MIAs largely rely on output probabilities or loss values and often perform only marginally better than random guessing when members and non-members are drawn from the same distribution. We introduce G-Drift MIA, a white-box membership inference method based on gradient-induced feature drift. Given a candidate (x,y), we apply a single targeted gradient-ascent step that increases its loss and measure the resulting changes in internal representations, including logits, hidden-layer activations, and projections onto fixed feature directions, before and after the update. These drift signals are used to train a lightweight logistic classifier that effectively separates members from non-members. Across multiple transformer-based LLMs and datasets derived from realistic MIA benchmarks, G-Drift substantially outperforms confidence-based, perplexity-based, and reference-based attacks. We further show that memorized training samples systematically exhibit smaller and more structured feature drift than non-members, providing a mechanistic link between gradient geometry, representation stability, and memorization. In general, our results demonstrate that small, controlled gradient interventions offer a practical tool for auditing the membership of training-data and assessing privacy risks in LLMs.

zh.title: G-Drift MIA：通过梯度诱导特征漂移进行LLM成员推断
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了G-Drift MIA，一种白盒成员推断方法，通过分析内部表示中的梯度诱导变化来确定数据是否被用于LLM训练。该方法大大优于现有攻击，并揭示了被记忆的样本表现出与非成员不同的特征漂移结构。

fr.title: G-Drift MIA : Inférence d'adhésion via le décalage de caractéristiques induit par gradient dans les LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté G-Drift MIA, une méthode d'inférence d'adhésion de boîte blanche qui détermine si les données ont été utilisées dans l'entraînement des LLM en analysant les modifications induites par gradient dans les représentations internes. La méthode surpasse considérablement les attaques existantes et révèle que les échantillons mémorisés présentent une dérive de caractéristiques structurellement différente des non-membres.

de.title: G-Drift MIA: Membership Inference via Gradient-induzierte Merkmalsabweichung in LLMs
de.summary: Forscher präsentierten G-Drift MIA, eine Whitebox-Methode zur Membership Inference, die bestimmt, ob Daten beim LLM-Training verwendet wurden, indem gradienteninduzierte Änderungen in internen Repräsentationen analysiert werden. Die Methode übertrifft bestehende Angriffe erheblich und zeigt, dass memorierte Proben strukturell unterschiedliche Merkmalsabweichungen aufweisen als Nicht-Mitglieder.

es.title: G-Drift MIA: Inferencia de membresía a través del cambio de características inducido por gradiente en LLMs
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron G-Drift MIA, un método de inferencia de membresía de caja blanca que determina si los datos se utilizaron en el entrenamiento de LLM analizando cambios inducidos por gradiente en representaciones internas. El método supera sustancialmente los ataques existentes y revela que las muestras memorizadas exhiben un cambio de características estructuralmente diferente que los no miembros.

title: Polysemanticity or Polysemy? Lexical Identity Confounds Superposition Metrics
summary: Researchers identified a significant lexical confound in standard superposition metrics: neurons firing for the same word form (like "bank") can be mistaken for semantic compression of unrelated concepts. Filtering out this confound improves downstream tasks like word sense disambiguation and makes knowledge edits more selective.
content:
arXiv:2604.00443v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: If the same neuron activates for both "lender" and "riverside," standard metrics attribute the overlap to superposition--the neuron must be compressing two unrelated concepts. This work explores how much of the overlap is due a lexical confound: neurons fire for a shared word form (such as "bank") rather than for two compressed concepts. A 2x2 factorial decomposition reveals that the lexical-only condition (same word, different meaning) consistently exceeds the semantic-only condition (different word, same meaning) across models spanning 110M-70B parameters. The confound carries into sparse autoencoders (18-36% of features blend senses), sits in <=1% of activation dimensions, and hurts downstream tasks: filtering it out improves word sense disambiguation and makes knowledge edits more selective (p = 0.002).

zh.title: 多义性还是多义？词汇同一性混淆叠加度量
zh.summary: 研究人员在标准叠加度量中发现了一个显著的词汇混淆：对同一词形（如"bank"）放电的神经元可能被误解为无关概念的语义压缩。过滤掉这种混淆改进了下游任务，如词义消歧，并使知识编辑更具选择性。

fr.title: Polysémantique ou Polysémie ? L'Identité Lexicale Confond les Métriques de Superposition
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont identifié une confusion lexicale significative dans les mesures de superposition standard : les neurones se déclenchant pour la même forme de mot (comme "bank") peuvent être confondus avec la compression sémantique de concepts non liés. Filtrer cette confusion améliore les tâches en aval comme la désambiguation du sens des mots et rend les modifications de connaissances plus sélectives.

de.title: Polysemantizität oder Polysemie? Lexikalische Identität verwirrt Überlagerungsmetriken
de.summary: Forscher identifizierten eine signifikante lexikalische Verwirrung in Standard-Überlagerungsmetriken: Neuronen, die für die gleiche Wortform (wie "bank") feuern, können mit semantischer Kompression nicht verwandter Konzepte verwechselt werden. Das Herausfiltern dieser Verwirrung verbessert nachgelagerte Aufgaben wie die Wortsinndisambiguierung und macht Wissenbearbeitungen selektiver.

es.title: ¿Polisemántica o Polisemia? La Identidad Léxica Confunde las Métricas de Superposición
es.summary: Los investigadores identificaron una confusión léxica significativa en métricas de superposición estándar: las neuronas que se disparan para la misma forma de palabra (como "bank") pueden confundirse con la compresión semántica de conceptos no relacionados. Filtrar esta confusión mejora tareas posteriores como la desambiguación del significado de palabras y hace que las ediciones de conocimiento sean más selectivas.

title: First Logit Boosting: Visual Grounding Method to Mitigate Object Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Researchers proposed First Logit Boosting (FLB), a training-free technique that reduces object hallucination in vision-language models by preserving visual information throughout generation. FLB achieves significant improvements with negligible inference overhead, making it practical for real-time multimodal systems.
content:
arXiv:2604.00455v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recent Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across various multimodal tasks that require understanding both visual and linguistic inputs. However, object hallucination -- the generation of nonexistent objects in answers -- remains a persistent challenge. Although several approaches such as retraining and external grounding methods have been proposed to mitigate this issue, they still suffer from high data costs or structural complexity. Training-free methods such as Contrastive Decoding (CD) are more cost-effective, avoiding additional training or external models, but still suffer from long-term decay, where visual grounding weakens and language priors dominate as the generation progresses. In this paper, we propose First Logit Boosting (FLB), a simple yet effective training-free technique designed to alleviate long-term decay in LVLMs. FLB stores the logit of the first generated token and adds it to subsequent token predictions, effectively mitigating long-term decay of visual information. We observe that FLB (1) sustains the visual information embedded in the first token throughout generation, and (2) suppresses hallucinated words through the stabilizing effect of the ``The'' token. Experimental results show that FLB significantly reduces object hallucination across various tasks, benchmarks, and backbone models. Notably, it causes negligible inference overhead, making it highly applicable to real-time multimodal systems. Code is available at https://github.com/jiwooha20/FLB

zh.title: 首次逻辑增强：减轻大型视觉语言模型中物体幻觉的视觉接地方法
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了首次逻辑增强（FLB），这是一种无需训练的技术，通过在整个生成过程中保留视觉信息来减少视觉语言模型中的物体幻觉。FLB实现了显著的改进，推理开销可忽略不计，使其适用于实时多模态系统。

fr.title: First Logit Boosting : Méthode d'Ancrage Visuel pour Atténuer les Hallucinations d'Objets dans les Grands Modèles Vision-Langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont proposé First Logit Boosting (FLB), une technique sans formation qui réduit les hallucinations d'objets dans les modèles vision-langage en préservant les informations visuelles tout au long de la génération. FLB réalise des améliorations significatives avec une surcharge d'inférence négligeable, ce qui la rend pratique pour les systèmes multimodaux en temps réel.

de.title: First Logit Boosting: Methode der visuellen Grundung zur Verminderung von Objekthalluzinationen in großen Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: Forscher schlugen First Logit Boosting (FLB) vor, eine trainingsfreie Technik, die Objekthalluzinationen in Vision-Language-Modellen reduziert, indem visuelle Informationen während der Generierung erhalten bleiben. FLB erzielt erhebliche Verbesserungen mit vernachlässigbarem Inferenz-Overhead, was es praktisch für Echtzeit-Multimodal-Systeme macht.

es.title: First Logit Boosting: Método de Anclaje Visual para Mitigar Alucinaciones de Objetos en Grandes Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores propusieron First Logit Boosting (FLB), una técnica sin entrenamiento que reduce las alucinaciones de objetos en modelos de visión-lenguaje al preservar la información visual durante la generación. FLB logra mejoras significativas con una sobrecarga de inferencia insignificante, lo que la hace práctica para sistemas multimodales en tiempo real.

title: Not My Truce: Personality Differences in AI-Mediated Workplace Negotiation
summary: A study of 267 participants found that personality traits significantly moderate the effectiveness of AI-driven negotiation coaching, with different personality profiles responding distinctly to theory-driven versus general AI systems. The findings suggest AI coaching should adapt support intensity to individual personality rather than assuming universal effectiveness.
content:
arXiv:2604.00464v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: AI-driven conversational coaching is increasingly used to support workplace negotiation, yet prior work assumes uniform effectiveness across users. We challenge this assumption by examining how individual differences, particularly personality traits, moderate coaching outcomes. We conducted a between-subjects experiment (N=267) comparing theory-driven AI (Trucey), general-purpose AI (Control-AI), and a traditional negotiation handbook (Control-NoAI). Participants were clustered into three profiles -- resilient, overcontrolled, and undercontrolled -- based on the Big-Five personality traits and ARC typology. Resilient workers achieved broad psychological gains primarily from the handbook, overcontrolled workers showed outcome-specific improvements with theory-driven AI, and undercontrolled workers exhibited minimal effects despite engaging with the frameworks. These patterns suggest personality as a predictor of readiness beyond stage-based tailoring: vulnerable users benefit from targeted rather than comprehensive interventions. The study advances understanding of personality-determined intervention prerequisites and highlights design implications for adaptive AI coaching systems that align support intensity with individual readiness, rather than assuming universal effectiveness.

zh.title: 不是我的休战：人工智能调解的工作场所谈判中的人格差异
zh.summary: 一项对267名参与者的研究发现，人格特征显著调节了人工智能驱动的谈判指导的有效性，不同的人格档案对理论驱动的系统与通用人工智能系统的反应不同。研究结果表明，人工智能指导应该根据个人性格调整支持强度，而不是假设通用的有效性。

fr.title: Pas Ma Trêve : Différences de Personnalité dans la Négociation de Lieu de Travail Médiatisée par l'IA
fr.summary: Une étude portant sur 267 participants a révélé que les traits de personnalité modèrent considérablement l'efficacité du coaching de négociation piloté par l'IA, avec des profils de personnalité différents réagissant différemment aux systèmes d'IA théoriques par rapport aux systèmes généraux. Les résultats suggèrent que le coaching par IA devrait adapter l'intensité du soutien à la personnalité individuelle plutôt que d'assumer une efficacité universelle.

de.title: Nicht mein Waffenstillstand: Persönlichkeitsunterschiede in KI-vermittelten Arbeitsplatzverhandlungen
de.summary: Eine Studie mit 267 Teilnehmern ergab, dass Persönlichkeitsmerkmale die Wirksamkeit von KI-gesteuertem Verhandlungscoaching erheblich moderieren, wobei verschiedene Persönlichkeitsprofile unterschiedlich auf theoriegesteuerte versus allgemeine KI-Systeme reagieren. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass KI-Coaching die Unterstützungsintensität an die individuelle Persönlichkeit anpassen sollte, anstatt universelle Wirksamkeit anzunehmen.

es.title: No es Mi Tregua: Diferencias de Personalidad en la Negociación de Lugar de Trabajo Mediada por IA
es.summary: Un estudio de 267 participantes encontró que los rasgos de personalidad moderan significativamente la efectividad del coaching de negociación impulsado por IA, con perfiles de personalidad diferentes que responden de manera distinta a sistemas basados en teoría versus sistemas de IA generales. Los hallazgos sugieren que el coaching de IA debe adaptar la intensidad del apoyo a la personalidad individual en lugar de asumir una efectividad universal.

title: Executing as You Generate: Hiding Execution Latency in LLM Code Generation
summary: Researchers introduced Eager, a parallel execution paradigm that executes code while LLMs generate it instead of following a serial workflow. Results show up to 99.9% reduction in non-overlapped execution latency and up to 55% improvement in end-to-end latency across multiple LLMs and benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2604.00491v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Current LLM-based coding agents follow a serial execution paradigm: the model first generates the complete code, then invokes an interpreter to execute it. This sequential workflow leaves the executor idle during generation and the generator idle during execution, resulting in unnecessary end-to-end latency. We observe that, unlike human developers, LLMs produce code tokens sequentially without revision, making it possible to execute code as it is being generated. We formalize this parallel execution paradigm, modeling it as a three-stage pipeline of generation, detection, and execution, and derive closed-form latency bounds that characterize its speedup potential and operating regimes. We then present Eager, a concrete implementation featuring AST-based chunking, dynamic batching with gated execution, and early error interruption. We evaluate Eager across four benchmarks, seven LLMs, and three execution environments. Results show that Eager reduces the non-overlapped execution latency by up to 99.9% and the end-to-end latency by up to 55% across seven LLMs and four benchmarks.

zh.title: 边生成边执行：在LLM代码生成中隐藏执行延迟
zh.summary: 研究人员介绍了Eager，一种并行执行范式，它在LLM生成代码时同时执行代码，而不是遵循串行工作流。结果表明，在多个LLM和基准测试中，非重叠执行延迟最多可减少99.9%，端到端延迟最多可改进55%。

fr.title: Exécution pendant la génération : masquer la latence d'exécution dans la génération de code LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté Eager, un paradigme d'exécution parallèle qui exécute le code pendant que les LLM le génèrent, au lieu de suivre un flux de travail série. Les résultats montrent une réduction jusqu'à 99,9% de la latence d'exécution non chevauchée et une amélioration jusqu'à 55% de la latence de bout en bout sur plusieurs LLM et repères.

de.title: Ausführung während der Generierung: Verbergen der Ausführungslatenz bei der LLM-Codegenerierung
de.summary: Forscher führten Eager ein, ein paralleles Ausführungsparadigma, das Code ausführt, während LLMs ihn generieren, anstatt einem seriellen Workflow zu folgen. Die Ergebnisse zeigen eine Reduzierung der nicht überlappten Ausführungslatenz um bis zu 99,9% und eine Verbesserung der End-to-End-Latenz um bis zu 55% über mehrere LLMs und Benchmarks hinweg.

es.title: Ejecución mientras se genera: ocultando latencia de ejecución en generación de código LLM
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron Eager, un paradigma de ejecución paralela que ejecuta código mientras los LLM lo generan en lugar de seguir un flujo de trabajo en serie. Los resultados muestran una reducción de hasta 99,9% en latencia de ejecución no superpuesta y una mejora de hasta 55% en latencia de extremo a extremo en múltiples LLM y benchmarks.

title: A Reasoning-Enabled Vision-Language Foundation Model for Chest X-ray Interpretation
summary: Researchers presented CheXOne, a vision-language model that interprets chest X-rays by generating both diagnostic predictions and explicit clinical reasoning traces. Trained on 14.7 million instruction samples, CheXOne achieved performance comparable to or better than resident-written reports in 55% of cases while providing interpretable explanations.
content:
arXiv:2604.00493v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Chest X-rays (CXRs) are among the most frequently performed imaging examinations worldwide, yet rising imaging volumes increase radiologist workload and the risk of diagnostic errors. Although artificial intelligence (AI) systems have shown promise for CXR interpretation, most generate only final predictions, without making explicit how visual evidence is translated into radiographic findings and diagnostic predictions. We present CheXOne, a reasoning-enabled vision-language model for CXR interpretation. CheXOne jointly generates diagnostic predictions and explicit, clinically grounded reasoning traces that connect visual evidence, radiographic findings, and these predictions. The model is trained on 14.7 million instruction and reasoning samples curated from 30 public datasets spanning 36 CXR interpretation tasks, using a two-stage framework that combines instruction tuning with reinforcement learning to improve reasoning quality. We evaluate CheXOne in zero-shot settings across visual question answering, report generation, visual grounding and reasoning assessment, covering 17 evaluation settings. CheXOne outperforms existing medical and general-domain foundation models and achieves strong performance on independent public benchmarks. A clinical reader study demonstrates that CheXOne-drafted reports are comparable to or better than resident-written reports in 55% of cases, while effectively addressing clinical indications and enhancing both report writing and CXR interpretation efficiency. Further analyses involving radiologists reveal that the generated reasoning traces show high clinical factuality and provide causal support for the final predictions, offering a plausible explanation for the performance gains. These results suggest that explicit reasoning can improve model performance, interpretability and clinical utility in AI-assisted CXR interpretation.

zh.title: 用于胸部X射线解释的推理能力视觉语言基础模型
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了CheXOne，一种视觉语言模型，通过生成诊断预测和明确的临床推理轨迹来解释胸部X射线。CheXOne在1470万个指令样本上进行了训练，在55%的病例中取得了与住院医生报告相当或更好的性能，同时提供了可解释的解释。

fr.title: Un modèle fondationnel vision-langage avec raisonnement pour l'interprétation des radiographies thoraciques
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté CheXOne, un modèle vision-langage qui interprète les radiographies thoraciques en générant à la fois des prédictions diagnostiques et des traces de raisonnement clinique explicites. Entraîné sur 14,7 millions d'exemples d'instructions, CheXOne a obtenu des performances comparables ou supérieures aux rapports rédigés par des résidents dans 55% des cas tout en fournissant des explications interprétables.

de.title: Ein Reasoning-fähiges Vision-Language-Grundlagenmodell zur Interpretation von Thorax-Röntgenaufnahmen
de.summary: Forscher präsentierten CheXOne, ein Vision-Language-Modell, das Thorax-Röntgenaufnahmen durch die Generierung von diagnostischen Vorhersagen und expliziten klinischen Begründungsspuren interpretiert. CheXOne wurde auf 14,7 Millionen Instruktionsbeispielen trainiert und erreichte eine Leistung, die mit von Assistenzärzten verfassten Berichten vergleichbar oder besser war, in 55% der Fälle, während es interpretierbare Erklärungen lieferte.

es.title: Un modelo de base de visión-lenguaje habilitado para razonamiento para la interpretación de radiografías de tórax
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron CheXOne, un modelo de visión-lenguaje que interpreta radiografías de tórax generando predicciones diagnósticas y trazas de razonamiento clínico explícitas. Entrenado en 14,7 millones de muestras de instrucciones, CheXOne logró un rendimiento comparable o mejor que los informes escritos por residentes en el 55% de los casos mientras proporcionaba explicaciones interpretables.

title: Towards Initialization-dependent and Non-vacuous Generalization Bounds for Overparameterized Shallow Neural Networks
summary: Researchers developed the first fully initialization-dependent complexity bounds for shallow neural networks with logarithmic width dependency, improving upon previous spectral norm approaches. These bounds provide non-vacuous generalization guarantees for overparameterized networks and achieve tightness up to constant factors.
content:
arXiv:2604.00505v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Overparameterized neural networks often show a benign overfitting property in the sense of achieving excellent generalization behavior despite the number of parameters exceeding the number of training examples. A promising direction to explain benign overfitting is to relate generalization to the norm of distance from initialization, motivated by the empirical observations that this distance is often significantly smaller than the norm itself. However, the existing initialization-dependent complexity analyses cannot fully exploit the power of initialization since the associated bounds depend on the spectral norm of the initialization matrix, which can scale as a square-root function of the width and are therefore not effective for overparameterized models. In this paper, we develop the first \emph{fully} initialization-dependent complexity bounds for shallow neural networks with general Lipschitz activation functions, which enjoys a logarithmic dependency on the width. Our bounds depend on the path-norm of the distance from initialization, which are derived by introducing a new peeling technique to handle the challenge along with the initialization-dependent constraint. We also develop a lower bound tight up to a constant factor. Finally, we conduct empirical comparisons and show that our generalization analysis implies non-vacuous bounds for overparameterized networks.

zh.title: 关于过参数化浅层神经网络的初始化相关和非平凡泛化界
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了第一个具有对数宽度依赖性的浅层神经网络的完全初始化相关复杂度界，改进了以前的谱范数方法。这些界为过参数化网络提供了非平凡的泛化保证，并在常数因子范围内实现了紧性。

fr.title: Vers des bornes de généralisation dépendantes de l'initialisation et non triviales pour les réseaux de neurones peu profonds surparamétrés
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé les premières bornes de complexité entièrement dépendantes de l'initialisation pour les réseaux de neurones peu profonds avec dépendance à la largeur logarithmique, améliorant les approches précédentes de normes spectrales. Ces bornes fournissent des garanties de généralisation non triviales pour les réseaux surparamétrés et atteignent une étanchéité jusqu'aux facteurs constants.

de.title: Zur Initialisierungsabhängigkeit und nicht-trivialen Verallgemeinerungsgrenzen für überparameterisierte flache neuronale Netze
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten die ersten vollständig initialisierungsabhängigen Komplexitätsgrenzen für flache neuronale Netze mit logarithmischer Breitenabhängigkeit, was frühere Spektralnorm-Ansätze verbessert. Diese Grenzen bieten nicht-triviale Verallgemeinerungsgarantien für überparameterisierte Netzwerke und erreichen Straffheit bis zu konstanten Faktoren.

es.title: Hacia límites de generalización dependientes de la inicialización y no triviales para redes neuronales poco profundas sobreparametrizadas
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron los primeros límites de complejidad completamente dependientes de la inicialización para redes neuronales poco profundas con dependencia de ancho logarítmico, mejorando enfoques anteriores de norma espectral. Estos límites proporcionan garantías de generalización no triviales para redes sobreparametrizadas y logran estanqueidad hasta factores constantes.

title: MOON3.0: Reasoning-aware Multimodal Representation Learning for E-commerce Product Understanding
summary: Researchers introduced MOON3.0, a reasoning-aware multimodal model that leverages LLMs to explicitly model fine-grained product attributes in e-commerce. Using joint contrastive and reinforcement learning, MOON3.0 demonstrates state-of-the-art zero-shot performance across downstream tasks.
content:
arXiv:2604.00513v2 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: With the rapid growth of e-commerce, exploring general representations rather than task-specific ones has attracted increasing attention. Although recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have driven significant progress in product understanding, they are typically employed as feature extractors that implicitly encode product information into global embeddings, thereby limiting their ability to capture fine-grained attributes. Therefore, we argue that leveraging the reasoning capabilities of MLLMs to explicitly model fine-grained product attributes holds significant potential. Nevertheless, achieving this goal remains non-trivial due to several key challenges: (i) long-context reasoning tends to dilute the model's attention to salient information in the raw input; (ii) supervised fine-tuning (SFT) primarily encourages rigid imitation, limiting the exploration of effective reasoning strategies; and (iii) fine-grained details are progressively attenuated during forward propagation. To address these issues, we propose MOON3.0, the first reasoning-aware MLLM-based model for product representation learning. Our method (1) employs a multi-head modality fusion module to adaptively integrate raw signals; (2) incorporates a joint contrastive and reinforcement learning framework to autonomously explore more effective reasoning strategies; and (3) introduces a fine-grained residual enhancement module to progressively preserve local details throughout the network. Additionally, we release a large-scale multimodal e-commerce benchmark MBE3.0. Experimentally, our model demonstrates state-of-the-art zero-shot performance across various downstream tasks on both our benchmark and public datasets.

zh.title: MOON3.0: 面向电商产品理解的推理感知多模态表示学习
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了MOON3.0，这是一个推理感知的多模态模型，利用大语言模型明确建模电商中的细粒度产品属性。通过联合对比学习和强化学习，MOON3.0在下游任务的零样本性能上达到了最先进的水平。

fr.title: MOON3.0 : Apprentissage de représentation multimodale consciente du raisonnement pour la compréhension des produits de commerce électronique
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté MOON3.0, un modèle multimodal conscient du raisonnement qui exploite les LLM pour modéliser explicitement les attributs fins des produits dans le commerce électronique. En utilisant l'apprentissage par contraste conjoint et l'apprentissage par renforcement, MOON3.0 démontre des performances de zéro-shot de pointe dans les tâches en aval.

de.title: MOON3.0: Reasoning-aware Multimodale Repräsentationslernfähigkeit für E-Commerce-Produktverständnis
de.summary: Forscher stellten MOON3.0 vor, ein reasoning-bewusstes multimодales Modell, das LLMs nutzt, um feingranulare Produktattribute im E-Commerce explizit zu modellieren. Mit gemeinsamem kontrastivem Lernen und verstärktem Lernen zeigt MOON3.0 hochmoderne Zero-Shot-Leistung bei nachgelagerten Aufgaben.

es.title: MOON3.0: Aprendizaje de Representación Multimodal Consciente del Razonamiento para la Comprensión de Productos de Comercio Electrónico
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron MOON3.0, un modelo multimodal consciente del razonamiento que aprovecha los LLM para modelar explícitamente atributos de productos de granularidad fina en el comercio electrónico. Usando aprendizaje contrastivo conjunto y aprendizaje por refuerzo, MOON3.0 demuestra un rendimiento de tiro cero de última generación en tareas posteriores.

title: MAESIL: Masked Autoencoder for Enhanced Self-supervised Medical Image Learning
summary: Researchers proposed MAESIL, a self-supervised learning framework that processes 3D medical images as unified volumes rather than independent 2D slices, capturing critical axial coherence often lost in traditional approaches. The framework achieves significant improvements in reconstruction metrics over existing methods like VAE and VQ-VAE.
content:
arXiv:2604.00514v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Training deep learning models for three-dimensional (3D) medical imaging, such as Computed Tomography (CT), is fundamentally challenged by the scarcity of labeled data. While pre-training on natural images is common, it results in a significant domain shift, limiting performance. Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) on unlabeled medical data has emerged as a powerful solution, but prominent frameworks often fail to exploit the inherent 3D nature of CT scans. These methods typically process 3D scans as a collection of independent 2D slices, an approach that fundamentally discards critical axial coherence and the 3D structural context. To address this limitation, we propose the autoencoder for enhanced self-supervised medical image learning(MAESIL), a novel self-supervised learning framework designed to capture 3D structural information efficiently. The core innovation is the 'superpatch', a 3D chunk-based input unit that balances 3D context preservation with computational efficiency. Our framework partitions the volume into superpatches and employs a 3D masked autoencoder strategy with a dual-masking strategy to learn comprehensive spatial representations. We validated our approach on three diverse large-scale public CT datasets. Our experimental results show that MAESIL demonstrates significant improvements over existing methods such as AE, VAE and VQ-VAE in key reconstruction metrics such as PSNR and SSIM. This establishes MAESIL as a robust and practical pre-training solution for 3D medical imaging tasks.

zh.title: MAESIL: 增强自监督医学图像学习的掩码自编码器
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了MAESIL，一个自监督学习框架，将三维医学图像作为统一体积处理，而不是独立的二维切片，捕获传统方法中常常丢失的关键轴向连贯性。该框架在重建指标上相比VAE和VQ-VAE等现有方法取得了显著改进。

fr.title: MAESIL : Autoencodeur masqué pour l'apprentissage autonome amélioré des images médicales
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont proposé MAESIL, un cadre d'apprentissage auto-supervisé qui traite les images médicales 3D comme des volumes unifiés plutôt que comme des tranches 2D indépendantes, capturant la cohérence axiale critique souvent perdue dans les approches traditionnelles. Le cadre réalise des améliorations significatives dans les métriques de reconstruction par rapport aux méthodes existantes comme VAE et VQ-VAE.

de.title: MAESIL: Maskierter Autoencoder für verbessertes selbstüberwachtes Lernen von medizinischen Bildern
de.summary: Forscher schlugen MAESIL vor, einen selbstüberwachten Lernrahmen, der 3D-Medizinbilder als einheitliche Volumen statt als unabhängige 2D-Scheiben verarbeitet und die kritische axiale Kohärenz erfasst, die bei traditionellen Ansätzen häufig verloren geht. Das Framework erreicht signifikante Verbesserungen bei Rekonstruktionsmetriken gegenüber bestehenden Methoden wie VAE und VQ-VAE.

es.title: MAESIL: Autocodificador Enmascarado para Aprendizaje Autosupervisado Mejorado de Imágenes Médicas
es.summary: Los investigadores propusieron MAESIL, un marco de aprendizaje autosupervisado que procesa imágenes médicas 3D como volúmenes unificados en lugar de cortes 2D independientes, capturando la coherencia axial crítica que a menudo se pierde en los enfoques tradicionales. El marco logra mejoras significativas en métricas de reconstrucción en comparación con métodos existentes como VAE y VQ-VAE.

title: Toward Optimal Sampling Rate Selection and Unbiased Classification for Precise Animal Activity Recognition
summary: Researchers developed IBA-Net, a network that improves classification accuracy for specific animal behaviors by adaptively using multiple sampling rates and addressing class imbalance through neural collapse principles. Tested on livestock activity recognition, IBA-Net consistently outperforms existing approaches across datasets.
content:
arXiv:2604.00517v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: With the rapid advancements in deep learning techniques, wearable sensor-aided animal activity recognition (AAR) has demonstrated promising performance, thereby improving livestock management efficiency as well as animal health and welfare monitoring. However, existing research often prioritizes overall performance, overlooking the fact that classification accuracies for specific animal behavioral categories may remain unsatisfactory. This issue typically stems from suboptimal sampling rates or class imbalance problems. To address these challenges and achieve high classification accuracy across all individual behaviors in farm animals, we propose a novel Individual-Behavior-Aware Network (IBA-Net). This network enhances the recognition of each specific behavior by simultaneously customizing features and calibrating the classifier. Specifically, considering that different behaviors require varying sampling rates to achieve optimal performance, we design a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)-based Feature Customization (MFC) module. This module adaptively fuses data from multiple sampling rates, capturing customized features tailored to various animal behaviors. Additionally, to mitigate classifier bias toward majority classes caused by class imbalance, we develop a Neural Collapse-driven Classifier Calibration (NC3) module. This module introduces a fixed equiangular tight frame (ETF) classifier during the classification stage, maximizing the angles between pair-wise classifier vectors and thereby improving the classification performance for minority classes. To validate the effectiveness of IBA-Net, we conducted experiments on three public datasets covering goat, cattle, and horse activity recognition. The results demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms existing approaches across all datasets.

zh.title: 面向精确动物活动识别的最优采样率选择和无偏分类
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了IBA-Net，一个通过自适应使用多个采样率和通过神经塌陷原理解决类不平衡问题来提高特定动物行为分类准确度的网络。在牲畜活动识别上进行测试，IBA-Net在各个数据集上始终优于现有方法。

fr.title: Vers une sélection optimale du taux d'échantillonnage et une classification non biaisée pour la reconnaissance précise des activités animales
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé IBA-Net, un réseau qui améliore la précision de la classification pour les comportements animaux spécifiques en utilisant de manière adaptative plusieurs taux d'échantillonnage et en résolvant le déséquilibre des classes grâce aux principes d'effondrement neuronal. Testé sur la reconnaissance d'activités animales d'élevage, IBA-Net surpasse constamment les approches existantes sur les ensembles de données.

de.title: Zur optimalen Wahl der Abtastrate und unverzerrten Klassifizierung für präzise Erkennung von Tieraktivitäten
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten IBA-Net, ein Netzwerk, das die Klassifizierungsgenauigkeit für spezifische Tierkörperbewegungen verbessert, indem es mehrere Abtastraten adaptiv verwendet und Klassenungleichgewicht durch Prinzipien des neuronalen Zusammenbruchs behebt. Getestet auf Erkennung von Viehaktivitäten übertrifft IBA-Net durchgehend bestehende Ansätze in verschiedenen Datensätzen.

es.title: Hacia la selección óptima de la velocidad de muestreo y la clasificación imparcial para el reconocimiento preciso de actividades animales
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron IBA-Net, una red que mejora la precisión de la clasificación para comportamientos animales específicos utilizando de forma adaptativa múltiples frecuencias de muestreo y abordando el desequilibrio de clases mediante principios de colapso neural. Probado en el reconocimiento de actividades ganaderas, IBA-Net supera consistentemente los enfoques existentes en todos los conjuntos de datos.

title: Think, Act, Build: An Agentic Framework with Vision Language Models for Zero-Shot 3D Visual Grounding
summary: Researchers proposed TAB, a dynamic agentic framework that performs 3D visual grounding by leveraging 2D vision-language models to resolve spatial semantics and multi-view geometry to reconstruct 3D structure. Operating directly on RGB-D streams, TAB significantly outperforms previous zero-shot methods and surpasses some supervised baselines.
content:
arXiv:2604.00528v2 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: 3D Visual Grounding (3D-VG) aims to localize objects in 3D scenes via natural language descriptions. While recent advancements leveraging Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have explored zero-shot possibilities, they typically suffer from a static workflow relying on preprocessed 3D point clouds, essentially degrading grounding into proposal matching. To bypass this reliance, our core motivation is to decouple the task: leveraging 2D VLMs to resolve complex spatial semantics, while relying on deterministic multi-view geometry to instantiate the 3D structure. Driven by this insight, we propose "Think, Act, Build (TAB)", a dynamic agentic framework that reformulates 3D-VG tasks as a generative 2D-to-3D reconstruction paradigm operating directly on raw RGB-D streams. Specifically, guided by a specialized 3D-VG skill, our VLM agent dynamically invokes visual tools to track and reconstruct the target across 2D frames. Crucially, to overcome the multi-view coverage deficit caused by strict VLM semantic tracking, we introduce the Semantic-Anchored Geometric Expansion, a mechanism that first anchors the target in a reference video clip and then leverages multi-view geometry to propagate its spatial location across unobserved frames. This enables the agent to "Build" the target's 3D representation by aggregating these multi-view features via camera parameters, directly mapping 2D visual cues to 3D coordinates. Furthermore, to ensure rigorous assessment, we identify flaws such as reference ambiguity and category errors in existing benchmarks and manually refine the incorrect queries. Extensive experiments on ScanRefer and Nr3D demonstrate that our framework, relying entirely on open-source models, significantly outperforms previous zero-shot methods and even surpasses fully supervised baselines.

zh.title: 思考、行动、构建：用于零样本3D视觉定位的视觉语言模型智能框架
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了TAB，一个动态智能框架，通过利用2D视觉语言模型来解决空间语义，以及多视角几何来重建3D结构，从而执行3D视觉定位。TAB直接在RGB-D流上运行，显著优于以前的零样本方法，并超越了一些有监督的基准。

fr.title: Penser, Agir, Construire : Un Cadre Agentique avec des Modèles de Langage Visuel pour l'Ancrage Visuel 3D Zéro-Shot
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont proposé TAB, un cadre agentique dynamique qui effectue l'ancrage visuel 3D en exploitant des modèles de langage visuel 2D pour résoudre la sémantique spatiale et la géométrie multi-vue pour reconstruire une structure 3D. Fonctionnant directement sur des flux RGB-D, TAB surpasse considérablement les méthodes zéro-shot précédentes et surpasse certaines lignes de base supervisées.

de.title: Denken, Handeln, Bauen: Ein Agentenrahmen mit Vision-Sprachmodellen zur Zero-Shot-3D-Visionsverankerung
de.summary: Forscher schlugen TAB vor, ein dynamisches Agentenframework, das 3D-Visionsverankerung durchführt, indem es 2D-Vision-Sprachmodelle nutzt, um räumliche Semantik zu lösen und Multi-View-Geometrie zur Rekonstruktion der 3D-Struktur zu verwenden. TAB arbeitet direkt auf RGB-D-Streams und übertrifft frühere Zero-Shot-Methoden erheblich und surpasses einige überwachte Baselines.

es.title: Pensar, Actuar, Construir: Un Marco Agéntico con Modelos de Lenguaje Visual para la Localización Visual 3D de Cero Disparo
es.summary: Los investigadores propusieron TAB, un marco agéntico dinámico que realiza la localización visual 3D aprovechando modelos de lenguaje visual 2D para resolver la semántica espacial y la geometría multivista para reconstruir la estructura 3D. Operando directamente en flujos RGB-D, TAB supera significativamente los métodos anteriores de cero disparo y supera algunos puntos de referencia supervisados.

title: Optimsyn: Influence-Guided Rubrics Optimization for Synthetic Data Generation
summary: Researchers introduced Optimsyn, which optimizes synthetic training data generation by using gradient-based influence estimation to measure how samples affect downstream model performance. The framework uses reinforcement learning to adapt rubrics based on target-model feedback, showing consistent improvements across domains.
content:
arXiv:2604.00536v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong downstream performance largely due to abundant supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data. However, high-quality SFT data in knowledge-intensive domains such as humanities, social sciences, medicine, law, and finance is scarce because expert curation is expensive, privacy constraints are strict, and label consistency is hard to ensure. Recent work uses synthetic data, typically by prompting a generator over domain documents and filtering outputs with handcrafted rubrics. Yet rubric design is expert-dependent, transfers poorly across domains, and is often optimized through a brittle heuristic loop of writing rubrics, synthesizing data, training, inspecting results, and manually guessing revisions. This process lacks reliable quantitative feedback about how a rubric affects downstream performance. We propose evaluating synthetic data by its training utility on the target model and using this signal to guide data generation. Inspired by influence estimation, we adopt an optimizer-aware estimator that uses gradient information to quantify each synthetic sample's contribution to a target model's objective on specific tasks. Our analysis shows that even when synthetic and real samples are close in embedding space, their influence on learning can differ substantially. Based on this insight, we propose an optimization-based framework that adapts rubrics using target-model feedback. We provide lightweight guiding text and use a rubric-specialized model to generate task-conditioned rubrics. Influence score is used as the reward to optimize the rubric generator with reinforcement learning. Experiments across domains, target models, and data generators show consistent improvements and strong generalization without task-specific tuning.

zh.title: Optimsyn：用于合成数据生成的影响引导评分标准优化
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了Optimsyn，它通过使用基于梯度的影响估计来优化合成训练数据生成，以衡量样本如何影响下游模型性能。该框架使用强化学习基于目标模型反馈来适应评分标准，在各个领域显示出一致的改进。

fr.title: Optimsyn : Optimisation des Rubriques Guidée par l'Influence pour la Génération de Données Synthétiques
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté Optimsyn, qui optimise la génération de données d'entraînement synthétiques en utilisant l'estimation d'influence basée sur le gradient pour mesurer comment les échantillons affectent les performances du modèle en aval. Le cadre utilise l'apprentissage par renforcement pour adapter les rubriques en fonction des commentaires du modèle cible, montrant des améliorations constantes dans tous les domaines.

de.title: Optimsyn: Einflussgelenkte Rubrik-Optimierung für die Generierung synthetischer Daten
de.summary: Forscher führten Optimsyn ein, das die Generierung synthetischer Trainingsdaten durch gradientenbasierte Einflussschätzung optimiert, um zu messen, wie Stichproben die Downstream-Modellleistung beeinflussen. Das Framework nutzt verstärkendes Lernen, um Rubriken basierend auf Zielmodell-Feedback anzupassen und zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen in allen Bereichen.

es.title: Optimsyn: Optimización de Rúbricas Guiada por Influencia para la Generación de Datos Sintéticos
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron Optimsyn, que optimiza la generación de datos de entrenamiento sintéticos mediante la estimación de influencia basada en gradientes para medir cómo las muestras afectan el rendimiento del modelo posterior. El marco utiliza aprendizaje por refuerzo para adaptar las rúbricas basadas en retroalimentación del modelo objetivo, mostrando mejoras consistentes en todos los dominios.

title: MATHENA: Mamba-based Architectural Tooth Hierarchical Estimator and Holistic Evaluation Network for Anatomy
summary: Researchers presented MATHENA, a unified framework leveraging Mamba's linear-complexity state space models for comprehensive dental diagnosis from X-rays. The system handles tooth detection, caries segmentation, anomaly detection, and developmental staging, achieving 93.78% mAP in tooth detection.
content:
arXiv:2604.00537v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Dental diagnosis from Orthopantomograms (OPGs) requires coordination of tooth detection, caries segmentation (CarSeg), anomaly detection (AD), and dental developmental staging (DDS). We propose Mamba-based Architectural Tooth Hierarchical Estimator and Holistic Evaluation Network for Anatomy (MATHENA), a unified framework leveraging Mamba's linear-complexity State Space Models (SSM) to address all four tasks. MATHENA integrates MATHE, a multi-resolution SSM-driven detector with four-directional Vision State Space (VSS) blocks for O(N) global context modeling, generating per-tooth crops. These crops are processed by HENA, a lightweight Mamba-UNet with a triple-head architecture and Global Context State Token (GCST). In the triple-head architecture, CarSeg is first trained as an upstream task to establish shared representations, which are then frozen and reused for downstream AD fine-tuning and DDS classification via linear probing, enabling stable, efficient learning. We also curate PARTHENON, a benchmark comprising 15,062 annotated instances from ten datasets. MATHENA achieves 93.78% mAP@50 in tooth detection, 90.11% Dice for CarSeg, 88.35% for AD, and 72.40% ACC for DDS.

zh.title: MATHENA：基于Mamba的牙齿结构分层估计器和解剖学整体评估网络
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了MATHENA，一个利用Mamba线性复杂度状态空间模型的统一框架，用于从X射线进行全面的牙科诊断。该系统处理牙齿检测、龋齿分割、异常检测和发育分期，在牙齿检测中达到93.78% mAP。

fr.title: MATHENA : Estimateur Hiérarchique de Dents Architecturales Basé sur Mamba et Réseau d'Évaluation Holistique pour l'Anatomie
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont présenté MATHENA, un cadre unifié exploitant les modèles d'espace d'état à complexité linéaire de Mamba pour un diagnostic dentaire complet à partir de radiographies. Le système gère la détection des dents, la segmentation des caries, la détection des anomalies et la classification du développement, atteignant 93,78% mAP dans la détection des dents.

de.title: MATHENA: Mamba-basierter architektonischer Zahnhierarchie-Schätzer und ganzheitliches Bewertungsnetzwerk für Anatomie
de.summary: Forscher präsentierten MATHENA, ein einheitliches Framework, das die linearen komplexen Zustandsraummodelle von Mamba für eine umfassende Zahndiagnose aus Röntgenaufnahmen nutzt. Das System verwaltet Zahnenerkennung, Kariesegementation, Anomalienerkennung und Entwicklungsstadienklassifizierung und erreicht 93,78% mAP bei der Zahnenerkennung.

es.title: MATHENA: Estimador Jerárquico de Dientes Arquitectónicos Basado en Mamba y Red de Evaluación Holística para la Anatomía
es.summary: Los investigadores presentaron MATHENA, un marco unificado que aprovecha los modelos de espacio de estado de complejidad lineal de Mamba para diagnóstico dental integral a partir de radiografías. El sistema maneja detección de dientes, segmentación de caries, detección de anomalías y clasificación del estadio de desarrollo, logrando 93,78% mAP en detección de dientes.

title: HabitatAgent: An End-to-End Multi-Agent System for Housing Consultation
summary: HabitatAgent is a multi-agent LLM system for housing consultation that combines specialized agents for memory management, retrieval, generation, and validation to provide reliable recommendations. The system achieves 95% accuracy on real user scenarios, substantially outperforming a baseline method's 75%, by enabling auditable reasoning and multi-constraint handling.
content:
arXiv:2604.00556v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Housing selection is a high-stakes and largely irreversible decision problem. We study housing consultation as a decision-support interface for housing selection. Existing housing platforms and many LLM-based assistants often reduce this process to ranking or recommendation, resulting in opaque reasoning, brittle multi-constraint handling, and limited guarantees on factuality.
  We present HabitatAgent, the first LLM-powered multi-agent architecture for end-to-end housing consultation. HabitatAgent comprises four specialized agent roles: Memory, Retrieval, Generation, and Validation. The Memory Agent maintains multi-layer user memory through internal stages for constraint extraction, memory fusion, and verification-gated updates; the Retrieval Agent performs hybrid vector--graph retrieval (GraphRAG); the Generation Agent produces evidence-referenced recommendations and explanations; and the Validation Agent applies multi-tier verification and targeted remediation. Together, these agents provide an auditable and reliable workflow for end-to-end housing consultation.
  We evaluate HabitatAgent on 100 real user consultation scenarios (300 multi-turn question--answer pairs) under an end-to-end correctness protocol. A strong single-stage baseline (Dense+Rerank) achieves 75% accuracy, while HabitatAgent reaches 95%.

zh.title: HabitatAgent：端到端的多智能体住房咨询系统
zh.summary: HabitatAgent是一个用于住房咨询的多智能体LLM系统，结合了用于记忆管理、检索、生成和验证的专门智能体，以提供可靠的建议。该系统在真实用户场景上实现了95%的准确率，大幅超越基线方法的75%，通过实现可审计的推理和多约束处理。

fr.title: HabitatAgent : Un Système Multi-Agents de Bout en Bout pour la Consultation en Logement
fr.summary: HabitatAgent est un système LLM multi-agents pour la consultation en logement qui combine des agents spécialisés pour la gestion de la mémoire, la récupération, la génération et la validation afin de fournir des recommandations fiables. Le système atteint une précision de 95 % sur des scénarios utilisateur réels, surpassant considérablement la méthode de base de 75 %, en permettant un raisonnement auditable et la gestion de contraintes multiples.

de.title: HabitatAgent: Ein End-to-End-Multi-Agenten-System für Wohnungsberatung
de.summary: HabitatAgent ist ein Multi-Agenten-LLM-System für Wohnungsberatung, das spezialisierte Agenten für Speicherverwaltung, Abruf, Generierung und Validierung kombiniert, um zuverlässige Empfehlungen zu geben. Das System erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 95 % in realen Benutzerszenarien und übertrifft die Baseline-Methode von 75 % erheblich, indem es nachvollziehbare Schlussfolgerungen und Multi-Constraint-Handling ermöglicht.

es.title: HabitatAgent: Un Sistema Multi-Agentes de Extremo a Extremo para Consultoría de Vivienda
es.summary: HabitatAgent es un sistema LLM multi-agentes para consultoría de vivienda que combina agentes especializados para gestión de memoria, recuperación, generación y validación para proporcionar recomendaciones confiables. El sistema logra una precisión del 95% en escenarios de usuarios reales, superando significativamente el método de base del 75%, al permitir razonamiento auditable y manejo de múltiples restricciones.

title: UniMixer: A Unified Architecture for Scaling Laws in Recommendation Systems
summary: UniMixer proposes a unified scaling architecture for recommendation systems that unifies attention-based, TokenMixer-based, and factorization-machine approaches through parameterized feature mixing. The framework improves scaling efficiency and introduces UniMixing-Lite for further compression while maintaining or improving performance.
content:
arXiv:2604.00590v2 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: In recent years, the scaling laws of recommendation models have attracted increasing attention, which govern the relationship between performance and parameters/FLOPs of recommenders. Currently, there are three mainstream architectures for achieving scaling in recommendation models, namely attention-based, TokenMixer-based, and factorization-machine-based methods, which exhibit fundamental differences in both design philosophy and architectural structure. In this paper, we propose a unified scaling architecture for recommendation systems, namely \textbf{UniMixer}, to improve scaling efficiency and establish a unified theoretical framework that unifies the mainstream scaling blocks. By transforming the rule-based TokenMixer to an equivalent parameterized structure, we construct a generalized parameterized feature mixing module that allows the token mixing patterns to be optimized and learned during model training. Meanwhile, the generalized parameterized token mixing removes the constraint in TokenMixer that requires the number of heads to be equal to the number of tokens. Furthermore, we establish a unified scaling module design framework for recommender systems, which bridges the connections among attention-based, TokenMixer-based, and factorization-machine-based methods. To further boost scaling ROI, a lightweight UniMixing module is designed, \textbf{UniMixing-Lite}, which further compresses the model parameters and computational cost while significantly improve the model performance. The scaling curves are shown in the following figure. Extensive offline and online experiments are conducted to verify the superior scaling abilities of \textbf{UniMixer}.

zh.title: UniMixer：推荐系统中缩放律的统一架构
zh.summary: UniMixer提出了一种用于推荐系统的统一缩放架构，通过参数化特征混合统一了基于注意力的、基于TokenMixer的和因子分解机的方法。该框架提高了缩放效率，并引入了UniMixing-Lite以进一步压缩，同时保持或改进性能。

fr.title: UniMixer : Une Architecture Unifiée pour les Lois d'Échelle dans les Systèmes de Recommandation
fr.summary: UniMixer propose une architecture de mise à l'échelle unifiée pour les systèmes de recommandation qui unifie les approches basées sur l'attention, basées sur TokenMixer et les approches par machines de factorisation par le biais du mélange de caractéristiques paramétrées.
