Le cadre améliore l'efficacité d'échelle et introduit UniMixing-Lite pour une compression supplémentaire tout en maintenant ou améliorant les performances.

de.title: UniMixer: Eine einheitliche Architektur für Skalierungsgesetze in Empfehlungssystemen
de.summary: UniMixer schlägt eine einheitliche Skalierungsarchitektur für Empfehlungssysteme vor, die aufmerksamkeitsbasierte, TokenMixer-basierte und Faktorisierungsmaschinen-Ansätze durch parametrisiertes Feature-Mixing vereinheitlicht. Das Framework verbessert die Skalierungseffizienz und führt UniMixing-Lite für weitere Komprimierung ein, während die Leistung beibehalten oder verbessert wird.

es.title: UniMixer: Una Arquitectura Unificada para Leyes de Escalado en Sistemas de Recomendación
es.summary: UniMixer propone una arquitectura de escalado unificada para sistemas de recomendación que unifica enfoques basados en atención, basados en TokenMixer y máquinas de factorización a través de la mezcla de características parametrizadas. El marco mejora la eficiencia de escalado e introduce UniMixing-Lite para mayor compresión mientras mantiene o mejora el desempeño.

title: Streaming Model Cascades for Semantic SQL
summary: This work presents adaptive cascade algorithms for streaming execution in semantic SQL, enabling cost-effective inference by routing rows through fast proxy models and escalating uncertain cases to expensive oracles. Both proposed algorithms achieve F1 > 0.95 across six production datasets with formal guarantees on quality metrics.
content:
arXiv:2604.00660v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern data warehouses extend SQL with semantic operators that invoke large language models on each qualifying row, but the per-row inference cost is prohibitive at scale. Model cascades reduce this cost by routing most rows through a fast proxy model and delegating uncertain cases to an expensive oracle. Existing frameworks, however, require global dataset access and optimize a single quality metric, limiting their applicability in distributed systems where data is partitioned across independent workers. We present two adaptive cascade algorithms designed for streaming, per-partition execution in which each worker processes its partition independently without inter-worker communication. SUPG-IT extends the SUPG statistical framework to streaming execution with iterative threshold refinement and joint precision-recall guarantees. GAMCAL replaces user-specified quality targets with a learned calibration model: a Generalized Additive Model maps proxy scores to calibrated probabilities with uncertainty quantification, enabling direct optimization of a cost-quality tradeoff through a single parameter. Experiments on six datasets in a production semantic SQL engine show that both algorithms achieve F1 > 0.95 on every dataset. GAMCAL achieves higher F1 per oracle call at cost-sensitive operating points, while SUPG-IT reaches a higher quality ceiling with formal guarantees on precision and recall.

zh.title: 用于语义SQL的流式模型级联
zh.summary: 这项工作提出了用于语义SQL流式执行的自适应级联算法，通过将行路由到快速代理模型并将不确定的情况提升到昂贵的预言机，实现成本有效的推理。两个提议的算法在六个生产数据集中实现了F1 > 0.95，并对质量指标有正式的保证。

fr.title: Cascades de Modèles de Diffusion en Continu pour SQL Sémantique
fr.summary: Ce travail présente des algorithmes de cascade adaptative pour l'exécution de diffusion en continu en SQL sémantique, permettant une inférence rentable en acheminant les lignes via des modèles proxy rapides et en escaladant les cas incertains vers des oracles coûteux. Les deux algorithmes proposés atteignent F1 > 0,95 sur six ensembles de données de production avec des garanties formelles sur les mesures de qualité.

de.title: Streaming-Modell-Kaskaden für Semantisches SQL
de.summary: Diese Arbeit präsentiert adaptive Kaskadenalgorithmen für die Streaming-Ausführung in semantischem SQL, die kosteneffektive Inferenz durch Weiterleitung von Zeilen über schnelle Proxy-Modelle und Eskalation unsicherer Fälle zu teuren Orakeln ermöglichen. Beide vorgeschlagenen Algorithmen erreichen F1 > 0,95 über sechs Produktionsdatensätze hinweg mit formalen Garantien für Qualitätsmetriken.

es.title: Cascadas de Modelos de Flujo Continuo para SQL Semántico
es.summary: Este trabajo presenta algoritmos de cascada adaptativa para la ejecución de flujo continuo en SQL semántico, permitiendo inferencia rentable mediante el enrutamiento de filas a través de modelos proxy rápidos y escalando casos inciertos a oráculos costosos. Ambos algoritmos propuestos logran F1 > 0.95 en seis conjuntos de datos de producción con garantías formales sobre métricas de calidad.

title: Procela: Epistemic Governance in Mechanistic Simulations Under Structural Uncertainty
summary: Procela is a Python framework for mechanistic simulations that test their own assumptions by allowing variables as epistemic authorities and mechanisms as competing causal units updated at runtime. Applied to antimicrobial resistance spread, the approach reduces error by 20.4% by detecting coverage decay and adapting to structural uncertainty.
content:
arXiv:2604.00675v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Mechanistic simulations typically assume fixed ontologies: variables, causal relationships, and resolution policies are static. This assumption fails when the true causal structure is contested or unidentifiable-as in antimicrobial resistance (AMR) spread, where contact, environmental, and selection ontologies compete. We introduce Procela, a Python framework where variables act as epistemic authorities that maintain complete hypothesis memory, mechanisms encode competing ontologies as causal units, and governance observes epistemic signals and mutates system topology at runtime. This is the first framework where simulations test their own assumptions. We instantiate Procela for AMR in a hospital network with three competing families. Governance detects coverage decay, policy fragility, and runs structural probes. Results show 20.4% error reduction and 69% cumulative regret improvement over baseline. All experiments are reproducible with full auditability. Procela establishes a new paradigm: simulations that model not only the world but their own modeling process, enabling adaptation under structural uncertainty.

zh.title: Procela: 结构不确定性下机械模拟中的认知论治理
zh.summary: Procela是一个用于机械模拟的Python框架，它通过将变量视为认知论权威，机制作为在运行时更新的竞争因果单元，来测试自身假设。应用于抗菌素耐药性传播，该方法通过检测覆盖衰减和适应结构不确定性，将误差减少了20.4%。

fr.title: Procela : Gouvernance épistémique dans les simulations mécanistes sous incertitude structurelle
fr.summary: Procela est un cadre Python pour les simulations mécanistes qui testent leurs propres hypothèses en permettant aux variables d'être des autorités épistémiques et aux mécanismes d'être des unités causales concurrentes mises à jour à l'exécution. Appliqué à la propagation de la résistance aux antimicrobiens, l'approche réduit l'erreur de 20,4 % en détectant la décroissance de la couverture et en s'adaptant à l'incertitude structurelle.

de.title: Procela: Epistemische Steuerung in mechanistischen Simulationen unter struktureller Unsicherheit
de.summary: Procela ist ein Python-Framework für mechanistische Simulationen, die ihre eigenen Annahmen testen, indem es Variablen als epistemische Autoritäten und Mechanismen als konkurrierende, zur Laufzeit aktualisierte kausale Einheiten ermöglicht. Das Verfahren reduziert den Fehler um 20,4 %, indem es Abdeckungsabbau erkennt und sich an strukturelle Unsicherheit anpasst, wenn es auf die Ausbreitung von Antibiotikaresistenzen angewendet wird.

es.title: Procela: Gobernanza epistémica en simulaciones mecanicistas bajo incertidumbre estructural
es.summary: Procela es un marco de Python para simulaciones mecanicistas que prueban sus propias suposiciones permitiendo que variables actúen como autoridades epistémicas y mecanismos como unidades causales competitivas actualizadas en tiempo de ejecución. Aplicado a la propagación de resistencia a los antimicrobianos, el enfoque reduce el error en un 20,4 % detectando la decadencia de cobertura y adaptándose a la incertidumbre estructural.

title: Internal APIs Are All You Need: Shadow APIs, Shared Discovery, and the Case Against Browser-First Agent Architectures
summary: Unbrowse accelerates agent interactions with websites by indexing first-party APIs ("shadow APIs") used by websites themselves rather than requiring repeated DOM inspection and reverse-engineering. The system achieves 3.6x mean speedup over browser automation with cached routes completing in under 100ms.
content:
arXiv:2604.00694v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Autonomous agents increasingly interact with the web, yet most websites remain designed for human browsers -- a fundamental mismatch that the emerging ``Agentic Web'' must resolve. Agents must repeatedly browse pages, inspect DOMs, and reverse-engineer callable routes -- a process that is slow, brittle, and redundantly repeated across agents. We observe that every modern website already exposes internal APIs (sometimes called \emph{shadow APIs}) behind its user interface -- first-party endpoints that power the site's own functionality. We present Unbrowse, a shared route graph that transforms browser-based route discovery into a collectively maintained index of these callable first-party interfaces. The system passively learns routes from real browsing traffic and serves cached routes via direct API calls. In a single-host live-web benchmark of equivalent information-retrieval tasks across 94 domains, fully warmed cached execution averaged 950\,ms versus 3{,}404\,ms for Playwright browser automation (3.6$\times$ mean speedup, 5.4$\times$ median), with well-cached routes completing in under 100\,ms. A three-path execution model -- local cache, shared graph, or browser fallback -- ensures the system is voluntary and self-correcting. A three-tier micropayment model via the x402 protocol charges per-query search fees for graph lookups (Tier~3), a one-time install fee for discovery documentation (Tier~1), and optional per-execution fees for site owners who opt in (Tier~2). All tiers are grounded in a necessary condition for rational adoption: an agent uses the shared graph only when the total fee is lower than the expected cost of browser rediscovery.

zh.title: 内部API就是您所需的全部：影子API、共享发现和反对浏览器优先代理架构的论证
zh.summary: Unbrowse通过索引网站本身使用的第一方API（"影子API"）而不是需要重复的DOM检查和逆向工程来加速代理与网站的交互。该系统相比浏览器自动化实现了3.6倍的平均速度提升，缓存路由在100毫秒内完成。

fr.title: Les API internes sont tout ce dont vous avez besoin : API fantômes, découverte partagée et argument contre les architectures d'agents basées sur le navigateur
fr.summary: Unbrowse accélère les interactions des agents avec les sites Web en indexant les API propriétaires (« API fantômes ») utilisées par les sites Web eux-mêmes plutôt que d'exiger une inspection répétée du DOM et une rétro-ingénierie. Le système réalise une accélération moyenne de 3,6x par rapport à l'automatisation du navigateur, les itinéraires mis en cache se complétant en moins de 100 ms.

de.title: Interne APIs sind alles, was Sie brauchen: Shadow APIs, gemeinsame Ermittlung und das Argument gegen browsergestützte Agent-Architekturen
de.summary: Unbrowse beschleunigt Agent-Interaktionen mit Websites durch Indexierung von First-Party-APIs („Shadow APIs"), die von den Websites selbst verwendet werden, anstatt wiederholte DOM-Inspektionen und Reverse-Engineering zu erfordern. Das System erreicht eine durchschnittliche 3,6-fache Beschleunigung gegenüber Browser-Automatisierung mit Cached-Routen, die in weniger als 100 ms abgeschlossen werden.

es.title: Las API internas son todo lo que necesita: API fantasma, descubrimiento compartido y el caso contra arquitecturas de agentes basadas en navegador
es.summary: Unbrowse acelera las interacciones de agentes con sitios web indexando API de primera parte (« API fantasma ») utilizadas por los propios sitios web en lugar de requerir inspección repetida del DOM e ingeniería inversa. El sistema logra una aceleración media de 3,6x sobre automatización de navegador con rutas en caché completándose en menos de 100 ms.

title: Learning to Hint for Reinforcement Learning
summary: HiLL is a framework that jointly trains a hinter policy and reasoner policy during reinforcement learning to address advantage collapse by generating adaptive hints for hard questions. The approach shows consistent improvements over GRPO and prior hint-based methods across multiple benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2604.00698v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely used for reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, but it often suffers from advantage collapse: when all rollouts in a group receive the same reward, the group yields zero relative advantage and thus no learning signal. For example, if a question is too hard for the reasoner, all sampled rollouts can be incorrect and receive zero reward. Recent work addresses this issue by adding hints or auxiliary scaffolds to such hard questions so that the reasoner produces mixed outcomes and recovers a non-zero update. However, existing hints are usually fixed rather than adapted to the current reasoner, and a hint that creates learning signal under the hinted input does not necessarily improve the no-hint policy used at test time. To this end, we propose Hint Learning for Reinforcement Learning (HiLL), a framework that jointly trains a hinter policy and a reasoner policy during RL. For each hard question, the hinter generates hints online conditioned on the current reasoner's incorrect rollout, allowing hint generation to adapt to the reasoner's evolving errors. We further introduce hint reliance, which measures how strongly correct hinted trajectories depend on the hint. We derive a transferability result showing that lower hint reliance implies stronger transfer from hinted success to no-hint success, and we use this result to define a transfer-weighted reward for training the hinter. Therefore, HiLL favors hints that not only recover informative GRPO groups, but also produce signals that are more likely to improve the original no-hint policy. Experiments across multiple benchmarks show that HiLL consistently outperforms GRPO and prior hint-based baselines, demonstrating the value of adaptive and transfer-aware hint learning for RL. The code is available at https://github.com/Andree-9/HiLL.

zh.title: 强化学习的学习提示
zh.summary: HiLL是一个框架，在强化学习期间联合训练提示策略和推理策略，通过为困难问题生成自适应提示来解决优势崩溃。该方法在多个基准上相比GRPO和先前的基于提示的方法表现出一致的改进。

fr.title: Apprendre à donner des indices pour l'apprentissage par renforcement
fr.summary: HiLL est un cadre qui entraîne conjointement une politique de suggesteur et une politique de raisonneur lors de l'apprentissage par renforcement pour résoudre l'effondrement des avantages en générant des indices adaptatifs pour les questions difficiles. L'approche montre des améliorations cohérentes par rapport à GRPO et aux méthodes précédentes basées sur les indices sur plusieurs benchmarks.

de.title: Hinweise für das Reinforcement Learning erlernen
de.summary: HiLL ist ein Framework, das während des Reinforcement Learning eine Hinweis-Richtlinie und eine Reasoning-Richtlinie gemeinsam trainiert, um den Vorteilskollaps durch die Generierung adaptiver Hinweise für schwierige Fragen zu beheben. Der Ansatz zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber GRPO und vorherigen hinweisgestützten Methoden über mehrere Benchmarks hinweg.

es.title: Aprender a dar pistas para el aprendizaje por refuerzo
es.summary: HiLL es un marco que entrena conjuntamente una política de sugeridor y una política de razonador durante el aprendizaje por refuerzo para abordar el colapso de ventaja generando pistas adaptativas para preguntas difíciles. El enfoque muestra mejoras consistentes sobre GRPO y métodos anteriores basados en pistas en múltiples puntos de referencia.

title: AutoEG: Exploiting Known Third-Party Vulnerabilities in Black-Box Web Applications
summary: AutoEG is a multi-agent framework for automatically generating exploits targeting known vulnerabilities in black-box web applications by extracting trigger logic and iteratively refining attacks through feedback. The system achieves 82.41% success rate across 660 exploitation tasks, vastly outperforming baselines.
content:
arXiv:2604.00704v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large-scale web applications are widely deployed with complex third-party components, inheriting security risks arising from component vulnerabilities. Security assessment is therefore required to determine whether such known vulnerabilities remain practically exploitable in real applications. Penetration testing is a widely adopted approach that validates exploitability by launching concrete attacks against known vulnerabilities in real-world black-box systems. However, existing approaches often fail to automatically generate reliable exploits, limiting their effectiveness in practical security assessment. This limitation mainly stems from two issues: (1) precisely triggering vulnerabilities with correct technical details, and (2) adapting exploits to diverse real-world deployment settings.
  In this paper, we propose AutoEG, a fully automated multi-agent framework for exploit generation targeting black-box web applications. AutoEG has two phases: First, AutoEG extracts precise vulnerability trigger logic from unstructured vulnerability information and encapsulates it into reusable trigger functions. Second, AutoEG uses trigger functions for concrete attack objectives and iteratively refines exploits through feedback-driven interaction with the target application. We evaluate AutoEG on 104 real-world vulnerabilities with 29 attack objectives, resulting in 660 exploitation tasks and 55,440 exploit attempts. AutoEG achieves an average success rate of 82.41%, substantially outperforming state-of-the-art baselines, whose best performance reaches only 32.88%.

zh.title: AutoEG：在黑盒Web应用中利用已知的第三方漏洞
zh.summary: AutoEG是一个多智能体框架，用于自动生成针对黑盒Web应用中已知漏洞的利用程序，通过提取触发逻辑并通过反馈迭代改进攻击。该系统在660个利用任务中实现了82.41%的成功率，大大超过了基线。

fr.title: AutoEG : Exploitation des vulnérabilités tierces connues dans les applications Web en boîte noire
fr.summary: AutoEG est un cadre multi-agents pour générer automatiquement des exploits ciblant les vulnérabilités connues dans les applications Web en boîte noire en extrayant la logique de déclenchement et en affinant les attaques de manière itérative via des retours. Le système atteint un taux de succès de 82,41% sur 660 tâches d'exploitation, dépassant largement les lignes de base.

de.title: AutoEG: Ausnutzung bekannter Sicherheitslücken von Drittanbietern in Black-Box-Webanwendungen
de.summary: AutoEG ist ein Multi-Agent-Framework zur automatischen Generierung von Exploits, die auf bekannte Sicherheitslücken in Black-Box-Webanwendungen abzielen, indem es Triggerfunktionen extrahiert und Angriffe durch Feedback iterativ verfeinert. Das System erreicht eine Erfolgsquote von 82,41% bei 660 Exploitationsaufgaben und übertrifft die Baselines erheblich.

es.title: AutoEG: Explotación de vulnerabilidades conocidas de terceros en aplicaciones web de caja negra
es.summary: AutoEG es un marco multiagente para generar automáticamente exploits dirigidos a vulnerabilidades conocidas en aplicaciones web de caja negra extrayendo la lógica de activación y refinando iterativamente los ataques a través de retroalimentación. El sistema logra una tasa de éxito del 82,41% en 660 tareas de explotación, superando enormemente las líneas de base.

title: To Memorize or to Retrieve: Scaling Laws for RAG-Considerate Pretraining
summary: This study systematically analyzes the trade-off between pretraining data scale and retrieval store size for RAG-augmented language models across model scales from 30M to 3B parameters. The findings reveal that optimal resource allocation between parametric and non-parametric knowledge depends strongly on model scale and task type.
content:
arXiv:2604.00715v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) improves language model (LM) performance by providing relevant context at test time for knowledge-intensive situations. However, the relationship between parametric knowledge acquired during pretraining and non-parametric knowledge accessed via retrieval remains poorly understood, especially under fixed data budgets. In this work, we systematically study the trade-off between pretraining corpus size and retrieval store size across a wide range of model and data scales. We train OLMo-2-based LMs ranging from 30M to 3B parameters on up to 100B tokens of DCLM data, while varying both pretraining data scale (1-150x the number of parameters) and retrieval store size (1-20x), and evaluate performance across a diverse suite of benchmarks spanning reasoning, scientific QA, and open-domain QA. We find that retrieval consistently improves performance over parametric-only baselines across model scales and introduce a three-dimensional scaling framework that models performance as a function of model size, pretraining tokens, and retrieval corpus size. This scaling manifold enables us to estimate optimal allocations of a fixed data budget between pretraining and retrieval, revealing that the marginal utility of retrieval depends strongly on model scale, task type, and the degree of pretraining saturation. Our results provide a quantitative foundation for understanding when and how retrieval should complement pretraining, offering practical guidance for allocating data resources in the design of scalable language modeling systems.

zh.title: 是否需要记忆或检索：RAG考虑预训练的缩放规律
zh.summary: 本研究系统分析了RAG增强语言模型中预训练数据规模与检索库大小之间的权衡，跨越从30M到3B参数的模型规模。研究结果表明，参数知识和非参数知识之间的最优资源分配强烈取决于模型规模和任务类型。

fr.title: Mémoriser ou récupérer : Lois d'échelle pour la préformation tenant compte de la RAG
fr.summary: Cette étude analyse systématiquement le compromis entre l'échelle des données de préformation et la taille du magasin de récupération pour les modèles de langage augmentés par RAG sur des échelles de modèle de 30 millions à 3 milliards de paramètres. Les résultats révèlent que l'allocation optimale des ressources entre la connaissance paramétrique et non paramétrique dépend fortement de l'échelle du modèle et du type de tâche.

de.title: Zu memorieren oder abzurufen: Skalierungsgesetze für RAG-bewusste Vortraining
de.summary: Diese Studie analysiert systematisch den Kompromiss zwischen der Skala der Vortrainingsdaten und der Größe des Abrufspeicherns für RAG-erweiterte Sprachmodelle über Modellskalen von 30M bis 3B Parametern. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass die optimale Ressourcenverteilung zwischen parametrischem und nicht-parametrischem Wissen stark von der Modellskala und dem Aufgabentyp abhängt.

es.title: Memorizar o recuperar: Leyes de escala para preentrenamiento que considera RAG
es.summary: Este estudio analiza sistemáticamente el equilibrio entre la escala de datos de preentrenamiento y el tamaño del almacén de recuperación para modelos de lenguaje aumentados por RAG en escalas de modelo de 30M a 3B parámetros. Los hallazgos revelan que la asignación óptima de recursos entre el conocimiento paramétrico y no paramétrico depende fuertemente de la escala del modelo y del tipo de tarea.

title: GRASP: Gradient Realignment via Active Shared Perception for Multi-Agent Collaborative Optimization
summary: GRASP addresses non-stationarity in multi-agent RL by enabling agents to actively perceive policy updates rather than passively sampling interactions, achieving consensus gradients based on generalized Bellman equilibrium. Experiments on SMAC and Google Research Football demonstrate improved scalability and faster convergence.
content:
arXiv:2604.00717v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Non-stationarity arises from concurrent policy updates and leads to persistent environmental fluctuations. Existing approaches like Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE) and sequential update schemes mitigate this issue. However, since the perception of the policies of other agents remains dependent on sampling environmental interaction data, the agent essentially operates in a passive perception state. This inevitably triggers equilibrium oscillations and significantly slows the convergence speed of the system. To address this issue, we propose Gradient Realignment via Active Shared Perception (GRASP), a novel framework that defines generalized Bellman equilibrium as a stable objective for policy evolution. The core mechanism of GRASP involves utilizing the independent gradients of agents to derive a defined consensus gradient, enabling agents to actively perceive policy updates and optimize team collaboration. Theoretically, we leverage the Kakutani Fixed-Point Theorem to prove that the consensus direction $u^*$ guarantees the existence and attainability of this equilibrium. Extensive experiments on StarCraft II Multi-Agent Challenge (SMAC) and Google Research Football (GRF) demonstrate the scalability and promising performance of the framework.

zh.title: GRASP：通过主动共享感知的多智能体协作优化梯度重新对齐
zh.summary: GRASP通过使智能体主动感知策略更新而非被动采样交互来解决多智能体强化学习中的非平稳性，实现基于广义贝尔曼均衡的共识梯度。在SMAC和Google Research Football上的实验证明了改进的可扩展性和更快的收敛速度。

fr.title: GRASP : Réalignement des gradients via la perception partagée active pour l'optimisation collaborative multi-agents
fr.summary: GRASP aborde la non-stationnarité dans l'RL multi-agents en permettant aux agents de percevoir activement les mises à jour de politique plutôt que d'échantillonner passivement les interactions, réalisant les gradients de consensus basés sur l'équilibre de Bellman généralisé. Les expériences sur SMAC et Google Research Football démontrent une meilleure scalabilité et une convergence plus rapide.

de.title: GRASP: Gradientenneujustierung durch aktive gemeinsame Wahrnehmung für Multi-Agent-Optimierung
de.summary: GRASP befasst sich mit der Nicht-Stationarität in Multi-Agent-RL, indem es Agenten ermöglicht, Richtlinienupdates aktiv wahrzunehmen, anstatt Interaktionen passiv abzutasten, und erreicht Konsensgradiente basierend auf verallgemeinertem Bellman-Gleichgewicht. Experimente auf SMAC und Google Research Football demonstrieren verbesserte Skalierbarkeit und schnellere Konvergenz.

es.title: GRASP: Realineación de gradientes mediante percepción compartida activa para optimización colaborativa multiagente
es.summary: GRASP aborda la no estacionariedad en RL multiagente al permitir que los agentes perciban activamente las actualizaciones de políticas en lugar de muestrear pasivamente interacciones, logrando gradientes de consenso basados en el equilibrio de Bellman generalizado. Los experimentos en SMAC y Google Research Football demuestran escalabilidad mejorada y convergencia más rápida.

title: A CEFR-Inspired Classification Framework with Fuzzy C-Means To Automate Assessment of Programming Skills in Scratch
summary: This framework automates assessment of Scratch programming skills using Fuzzy C-Means clustering aligned with CEFR competency levels (A1-C2) across 2 million projects. Results reveal a "B2 bottleneck" where learners struggle with logic synchronization and data representation.
content:
arXiv:2604.00730v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Context: Schools, training platforms, and technology firms increasingly need to assess programming proficiency at scale with transparent, reproducible methods that support personalized learning pathways. Objective: This study introduces a pedagogical framework for Scratch project assessment, aligned with the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR), providing universal competency levels for students and teachers alongside actionable insights for curriculum design. Method: We apply Fuzzy C-Means clustering to 2008246 Scratch projects evaluated via Dr.Scratch, implementing an ordinal criterion to map clusters to CEFR levels (A1-C2), and introducing enhanced classification metrics that identify transitional learners, enable continuous progress tracking, and quantify classification certainty to balance automated feedback with instructor review. Impact: The framework enables diagnosis of systemic curriculum gaps-notably a "B2 bottleneck" where only 13.3% of learners reside due to the cognitive load of integrating Logic Synchronization, and Data Representation--while providing certainty--based triggers for human intervention.

zh.title: 一个受CEFR启发的模糊C均值分类框架用于自动化评估Scratch编程技能
zh.summary: 该框架使用与CEFR能力水平（A1-C2）相对应的模糊C均值聚类来自动化评估Scratch编程技能，涵盖200万个项目。结果揭示了一个"B2瓶颈"，学习者在逻辑同步和数据表示方面遇到困难。

fr.title: Un cadre de classification inspiré du CECR avec clustering C-means flou pour automatiser l'évaluation des compétences en programmation Scratch
fr.summary: Ce cadre automatise l'évaluation des compétences en programmation Scratch en utilisant le clustering C-means flou aligné sur les niveaux de compétence du CECR (A1-C2) sur 2 millions de projets. Les résultats révèlent un "goulot d'étranglement B2" où les apprenants ont du mal avec la synchronisation logique et la représentation des données.

de.title: Ein CEFR-inspiriertes Klassifizierungsrahmenwerk mit Fuzzy-C-Means zur automatisierten Bewertung von Scratch-Programmierkompetenzen
de.summary: Dieses Framework automatisiert die Bewertung von Scratch-Programmierkompetenzen mit Fuzzy-C-Means-Clustering, das an CEFR-Kompetenzstufen (A1-C2) ausgerichtet ist, über 2 Millionen Projekte hinweg. Die Ergebnisse zeigen einen "B2-Engpass", bei dem Lernende bei der logischen Synchronisierung und Datendarstellung kämpfen.

es.title: Un marco de clasificación inspirado en MCER con agrupamiento difuso C-Means para automatizar la evaluación de habilidades de programación en Scratch
es.summary: Este marco automatiza la evaluación de habilidades de programación en Scratch utilizando agrupamiento C-Means difuso alineado con los niveles de competencia del MCER (A1-C2) en 2 millones de proyectos. Los resultados revelan un "cuello de botella B2" donde los estudiantes luchan con la sincronización lógica y la representación de datos.

title: Spectral Compact Training: Pre-Training Large Language Models via Permanent Truncated SVD and Stiefel QR Retraction
summary: Spectral Compact Training enables memory-efficient LLM pretraining by replacing dense weight matrices with permanently truncated SVD factors that are never fully materialized, achieving up to 199x memory reduction. The method enables training 70B-parameter models on consumer hardware like a Steam Deck.
content:
arXiv:2604.00733v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The memory wall remains the primary bottleneck for training large language models on consumer hardware. We introduce Spectral Compact Training (SCT), a method that replaces dense weight matrices with permanent truncated SVD factors W = U diag(s) V^T, where the full dense matrix is never materialized during training or inference. Gradients flow through the compact spectral factors via standard backpropagation, and U, V are retracted to the Stiefel manifold via QR decomposition after each optimizer step. SCT achieves up to 199x memory reduction per MLP layer at rank 32, enabling full training steps of 70B-parameter architectures on a Steam Deck handheld (7.2 GB peak memory vs. 1,245 GB for dense FP32 training with Adam). Rank-sweep experiments on SmolLM2-1.7B (ranks 32-256, 2000 steps, NVIDIA A100) show that all tested ranks converge to the same loss floor (~4.2-4.5), identifying the learning rate schedule -- not MLP rank -- as the primary bottleneck. Rank 128 emerges as the efficiency sweet spot at 11.7x MLP compression with the lowest perplexity. GPU memory drops 46% at rank 32 while training throughput doubles.

zh.title: 频谱紧凑训练：通过永久截断SVD和Stiefel QR回缩进行大型语言模型预训练
zh.summary: 频谱紧凑训练通过用永久截断的SVD因子替换密集权重矩阵来实现内存高效的LLM预训练，这些因子永远不会完全实现，可达到199倍的内存减少。该方法可在Steam Deck等消费级硬件上训练70B参数模型。

fr.title: Entraînement spectral compact : pré-entraînement de grands modèles de langage via SVD tronqué permanent et rétraction QR de Stiefel
fr.summary: L'entraînement spectral compact permet un pré-entraînement LLM économe en mémoire en remplaçant les matrices de poids denses par des facteurs SVD tronqués permanents qui ne sont jamais complètement matérialisés, réalisant jusqu'à 199x de réduction mémoire. La méthode permet l'entraînement de modèles à 70B paramètres sur du matériel grand public comme une Steam Deck.

de.title: Spektrales kompaktes Training: Vortraining großer Sprachmodelle mittels permanenter verkürzter SVD und Stiefel-QR-Retraktion
de.summary: Das spektrale kompakte Training ermöglicht speichereffizientes LLM-Vortraining, indem dichte Gewichtsmatrizen durch permanent verkürzte SVD-Faktoren ersetzt werden, die nie vollständig materialisiert werden, und eine bis zu 199-fache Speicherreduktion erreichen. Die Methode ermöglicht das Training von Modellen mit 70B-Parametern auf Consumer-Hardware wie einem Steam Deck.

es.title: Entrenamiento espectral compacto: pre-entrenamiento de grandes modelos de lenguaje a través de SVD truncado permanente y retracción QR de Stiefel
es.summary: El entrenamiento espectral compacto permite el pre-entrenamiento de LLM eficiente en memoria al reemplazar matrices de peso densas con factores SVD truncados permanentemente que nunca se materializan completamente, logrando una reducción de memoria de hasta 199x. El método permite entrenar modelos de parámetros 70B en hardware de consumidor como una Steam Deck.

title: BioCOMPASS: Integrating Biomarkers into Transformer-Based Immunotherapy Response Prediction
summary: BioCOMPASS extends transformer-based immunotherapy response prediction by integrating biomarkers and treatment information through aligned loss components rather than direct input features. The approach improves generalizability across diverse cohorts, cancer types, and treatment variations.
content:
arXiv:2604.00739v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Datasets used in immunotherapy response prediction are typically small in size, as well as diverse in cancer type, drug administered, and sequencer used. Models often drop in performance when tested on patient cohorts that are not included in the training process. Recent work has shown that transformer-based models along with self-supervised learning show better generalisation performance than threshold-based biomarkers, but is still suboptimal. We present BioCOMPASS, an extension of a transformer-based model called COMPASS, that integrates biomarkers and treatment information to further improve its generalisability. Instead of feeding biomarker data as input, we built loss components to align them with the model's intermediate representations. We found that components such as treatment gating and pathway consistency loss improved generalisability when evaluated with Leave-one-cohort-out, Leave-one-cancer-type-out and Leave-one-treatment-out strategies. Results show that building components that exploit biomarker and treatment information can help in generalisability of immunotherapy response prediction. Careful curation of additional components that leverage complementary clinical information and domain knowledge represents a promising direction for future research.

zh.title: BioCOMPASS：将生物标志物集成到基于Transformer的免疫治疗反应预测中
zh.summary: BioCOMPASS通过对齐损失分量而不是直接输入特征来扩展基于Transformer的免疫治疗反应预测，集成生物标志物和治疗信息。该方法改进了在不同队列、癌症类型和治疗变异中的可泛化性。

fr.title: BioCOMPASS : Intégration de biomarqueurs dans la prédiction de réponse à l'immunothérapie basée sur Transformer
fr.summary: BioCOMPASS étend la prédiction de réponse à l'immunothérapie basée sur Transformer en intégrant des biomarqueurs et des informations de traitement via des composantes de perte alignées plutôt que des caractéristiques d'entrée directes. L'approche améliore la généralisation à travers des cohortes diverses, des types de cancer et des variations de traitement.

de.title: BioCOMPASS: Integration von Biomarkern in die Transformer-basierte Immuntherapie-Reaktionsprognose
de.summary: BioCOMPASS erweitert die Transformer-basierte Immuntherapie-Reaktionsprognose durch die Integration von Biomarkern und Behandlungsinformationen durch ausgerichtete Verlustkomponenten anstelle von direkten Eingabefunktionen. Der Ansatz verbessert die Verallgemeinerbarkeit über verschiedene Kohorten, Krebsarten und Behandlungsvariationen.

es.title: BioCOMPASS: Integración de biomarcadores en la predicción de respuesta a inmunoterapia basada en Transformer
es.summary: BioCOMPASS extiende la predicción de respuesta a la inmunoterapia basada en Transformer al integrar biomarcadores e información de tratamiento a través de componentes de pérdida alineados en lugar de características de entrada directa. El enfoque mejora la generalizabilidad entre cohortes diversas, tipos de cáncer y variaciones de tratamiento.

title: IWP: Token Pruning as Implicit Weight Pruning in Large Vision Language Models
summary: IWP reformulates token pruning for vision-language models through a dual form perspective on attention, where pruning selects optimal rank-1 token contributions. The training-free method achieves better performance-efficiency trade-offs while providing interpretability into existing pruning approaches.
content:
arXiv:2604.00757v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Vision Language Models show impressive performance across image and video understanding tasks, yet their computational cost grows rapidly with the number of visual tokens. Existing token pruning methods mitigate this issue through empirical approaches while overlooking the internal mechanism of attention. In this paper, we propose a novel training free token pruning framework grounded in the dual form perspective of attention. We reformulate attention as an implicit linear layer whose weight matrix is the sum of rank 1 outer products, each generated by a single token's key value pair. Token pruning thus reduces to selecting an optimal subset of these rank 1 updates that best approximates the original dual weight matrix. Extending this perspective to standard softmax attention in LVLMs, we derive a novel metric quantifying both a token's information magnitude and information duplication. To efficiently select the subset with the proposed metric, we introduce Progressive Chunked Maximal Marginal Relevance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves a better trade off between performance and efficiency, while providing another perspective on existing pruning approaches.

zh.title: IWP：大型视觉语言模型中作为隐式权重剪枝的令牌剪枝
zh.summary: IWP通过关注机制的对偶形式角度重新制定了视觉语言模型的令牌剪枝，其中剪枝选择最优的秩-1令牌贡献。这种无需训练的方法实现了更好的性能-效率权衡，同时提供了对现有剪枝方法的可解释性。

fr.title: IWP : L'élagage des jetons comme élagage implicite des poids dans les grands modèles de vision-langage
fr.summary: IWP reformule l'élagage des jetons pour les modèles vision-langage par une perspective de forme duelle sur l'attention, où l'élagage sélectionne les contributions optimales des jetons de rang 1. La méthode sans entraînement réalise de meilleurs compromis performance-efficacité tout en fournissant une interprétabilité des approches d'élagage existantes.

de.title: IWP: Token-Pruning als implizites Gewichtspruning in großen Vision-Language-Modellen
de.summary: IWP reformuliert das Token-Pruning für Vision-Language-Modelle durch eine duale Formperspektive auf Aufmerksamkeit, wobei Pruning optimale Rang-1-Token-Beiträge auswählt. Die trainingsfreie Methode erreicht bessere Leistungs-Effizienz-Tradeoffs und bietet gleichzeitig Interpretierbarkeit für bestehende Pruning-Ansätze.

es.title: IWP: Poda de tokens como poda de pesos implícita en grandes modelos de visión-lenguaje
es.summary: IWP reformula la poda de tokens para modelos de visión-lenguaje a través de una perspectiva de forma dual en atención, donde la poda selecciona contribuciones óptimas de tokens de rango 1. El método sin entrenamiento logra mejores compensaciones entre rendimiento y eficiencia mientras proporciona interpretabilidad en los enfoques de poda existentes.

title: Thinking Wrong in Silence: Backdoor Attacks on Continuous Latent Reasoning
summary: ThoughtSteer demonstrates backdoor attacks on models with continuous latent reasoning by perturbing a single input embedding, achieving 99%+ attack success while evading token-level defenses and surviving fine-tuning. The work reveals that adversarial information exists in collective latent trajectories rather than individual vectors.
content:
arXiv:2604.00770v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: A new generation of language models reasons entirely in continuous hidden states, producing no tokens and leaving
  no audit trail. We show that this silence creates a fundamentally new attack surface. ThoughtSteer perturbs a
  single embedding vector at the input layer; the model's own multi-pass reasoning amplifies this perturbation into a
  hijacked latent trajectory that reliably produces the attacker's chosen answer, while remaining structurally
  invisible to every token-level defense. Across two architectures (Coconut and SimCoT), three reasoning benchmarks,
  and model scales from 124M to 3B parameters, ThoughtSteer achieves >=99% attack success rate with near-baseline
  clean accuracy, transfers to held-out benchmarks without retraining (94-100%), evades all five evaluated active
  defenses, and survives 25 epochs of clean fine-tuning. We trace these results to a unifying mechanism: Neural
  Collapse in the latent space pulls triggered representations onto a tight geometric attractor, explaining both why
  defenses fail and why any effective backdoor must leave a linearly separable signature (probe AUC>=0.999). Yet a
  striking paradox emerges: individual latent vectors still encode the correct answer even as the model outputs the
  wrong one. The adversarial information is not in any single vector but in the collective trajectory, establishing
  backdoor perturbations as a new lens for mechanistic interpretability of continuous reasoning. Code and checkpoints
  are available.

zh.title: 无声地思维错误：对连续潜在推理的后门攻击
zh.summary: ThoughtSteer通过扰动单个输入嵌入演示了对具有连续潜在推理的模型的后门攻击，实现了99%以上的攻击成功率，同时规避了令牌级防御并在微调后存活。这项工作揭示了对抗性信息存在于集合潜在轨迹中，而不是单个向量中。

fr.title: Penser à tort en silence : Attaques de porte dérobée sur le raisonnement latent continu
fr.summary: ThoughtSteer démontre les attaques de porte dérobée sur les modèles avec raisonnement latent continu en perturbant un seul plongement d'entrée, réalisant un succès d'attaque de 99%+ tout en évitant les défenses au niveau des jetons et en survivant aux ajustements fins. Le travail révèle que l'information adverse existe dans les trajectoires latentes collectives plutôt que dans les vecteurs individuels.

de.title: Still falsch denken: Backdoor-Attacken auf kontinuierliches latentes Denken
de.summary: ThoughtSteer demonstriert Backdoor-Attacken auf Modelle mit kontinuierlichem latentem Denken durch Störung eines einzelnen Eingabe-Embeddings und erreicht über 99% Attackenerfolg, während es Token-Level-Defenses umgeht und Feinabstimmung übersteht. Die Arbeit zeigt, dass gegnerische Informationen in kollektiven latenten Trajektorien statt in einzelnen Vektoren existieren.

es.title: Pensar mal en silencio: Ataques de puerta trasera en razonamiento latente continuo
es.summary: ThoughtSteer demuestra ataques de puerta trasera en modelos con razonamiento latente continuo perturbando una única incrustación de entrada, logrando éxito de ataque del 99%+ mientras evadir defensas a nivel de token y sobreviviendo al ajuste fino. El trabajo revela que la información adversarial existe en trayectorias latentes colectivas en lugar de vectores individuales.

title: Scalable Pretraining of Large Mixture of Experts Language Models on Aurora Super Computer
summary: This work showcases large-scale LLM pretraining on Aurora exascale supercomputer, training dense and Mixture of Experts models up to 220B parameters across thousands of GPU tiles. Novel optimizations achieved 90% scaling efficiency and 1.71x training speedups through custom kernels and EP-aware sharding.
content:
arXiv:2604.00785v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Pretraining Large Language Models (LLMs) from scratch requires massive amount of compute. Aurora super computer is an ExaScale machine with 127,488 Intel PVC (Ponte Vechio) GPU tiles. In this work, we showcase LLM pretraining on Aurora at the scale of 1000s of GPU tiles. Towards this effort, we developed Optimus, an inhouse training library with support for standard large model training techniques. Using Optimus, we first pretrained Mula-1B, a 1 Billion dense model and Mula-7B-A1B, a 7 Billion Mixture of Experts (MoE) model from scratch on 3072 GPU tiles for the full 4 trillion tokens of the OLMoE-mix-0924 dataset. We then demonstrated model scaling by pretraining three large MoE models Mula-20B-A2B, Mula-100B-A7B, and Mula-220B-A10B till 100 Billion tokens on the same dataset. On our largest model Mula-220B-A10B, we pushed the compute scaling from 384 to 12288 GPU tiles and observed scaling efficiency of around 90% at 12288 GPU tiles. We significantly improved the runtime performance of MoE models using custom GPU kernels for expert computation, and a novel EP-Aware sharded optimizer resulting in training speedups up to 1.71x. As part of the Optimus library, we also developed a robust set of reliability and fault tolerant features to improve training stability and continuity at scale.

zh.title: 在Aurora超级计算机上对大型专家混合语言模型的可扩展预训练
zh.summary: 这项工作展示了在Aurora超级计算机上的大规模LLM预训练，在数千个GPU磁贴上训练密集和专家混合模型，参数量高达220B。通过定制内核和感知EP分片的优化实现了90%的缩放效率和1.71倍的训练加速。

fr.title: Préentraînement évolutif de grands modèles de langage de mélange d'experts sur le supercalculateur Aurora
fr.summary: Ce travail présente le préentraînement à grande échelle des LLM sur le supercalculateur Aurora, entraînant des modèles denses et Mélange d'Experts jusqu'à 220B de paramètres sur des milliers de tuiles GPU. Les optimisations innovantes ont réalisé une efficacité d'échelle de 90% et des accélérations d'entraînement de 1,71x grâce aux noyaux personnalisés et au sharding tenant compte des EP.

de.title: Skalierbares Vortraining großer Mixture-of-Experts-Sprachmodelle auf dem Aurora-Supercomputer
de.summary: Diese Arbeit zeigt großflächiges LLM-Vortraining auf dem Aurora-Supercomputer, wobei dichte und Mixture-of-Experts-Modelle mit bis zu 220B Parametern auf Tausenden von GPU-Kacheln trainiert werden. Neuartige Optimierungen erreichten 90% Skalierungseffizienz und 1,71x Trainings-Speedups durch benutzerdefinierte Kernel und EP-bewusste Sharding.

es.title: Preentrenamiento escalable de grandes modelos de lenguaje de mezcla de expertos en la supercomputadora Aurora
es.summary: Este trabajo presenta el preentrenamiento a gran escala de LLM en la supercomputadora Aurora, entrenando modelos densos y Mezcla de Expertos de hasta 220B de parámetros en miles de tiles de GPU. Las optimizaciones novedosas lograron una eficiencia de escalado del 90% y aceleraciones de entrenamiento de 1,71x a través de kernels personalizados y sharding consciente de EP.
